Where the rising\nor tumult is merely to accomplish some private\npurpose, interesting only to those engaged in it,\nand not resisting or calling in question the King&#x27;s\nauthority or prerogative then the tumult, however\nnumerous or outrageous the mob may be, is held\nonly to be a riot. For example, suppose a mob to\nrise, and even by force of arms to break into a\nparticular prison and rescue certain persons\ntherein confined, or to oblige the Magistrates to\nset them at liberty or to lower the price of\nprovisions in a certain market, or to tear down\ncertain    enclosures,    which     they    conceive   to\nencroach on the town&#x27;s commons. All such acts,\nthough severely punishable, and though they\nmay be resisted by force, do not amount to\ntreason. Nothing is pointed against either the\nperson or authority of the King&quot;.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_47\">&quot;But,     gentlemen,     wherever     the    rising    or\ninsurrection has for its object a general purpose,\nnot confined to the peculiar views and interests of\nthe persons concerned in it, but common to the\nwhole community, and striking directly the King&#x27;s\nauthority or that of Parliament, then it assumes\nthe character of treason. For example, if mobs\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_80\">                                 77</span>\n\n\n\n\n      were to rise in different parts of the country to\n      throw open all enclosures and to resist the\n      execution   of   the   law     regarding   enclosures\n      wheresoever attempted, to pull down all prisons\n      or Courts of justice, to resist all revenue officers in\n      the collecting of all or any of the taxes; in short,\n      all risings to accomplish a general purpose, or to\n      hinder a general measure, which by law can only\n      be authorized or prohibited by authority of the\n      King or Parliament, amount to levying of war\n      against the King and have always been tried and\n      punished as treason. It is, therefore, not the\n      numbers concerned, nor the force employed by\n      the people rising in arms, but the object which\n      they have in view that determines the character\n      of the crime, and will make it either riot or\n      treason, according as that object is of a public\n      and general, or private and local nature&quot;.\n</blockquote>\n\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_140\">75.         Then in 1839, TINDAL, C. J. while summing up\n\nthe Jury in the trial of John Frost in the year 1839 [All ER\n\nReprint 1835-1842 P.106 at P.117] stated that it was:\n\n\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_48\">      &quot;essential to the making out of the charge of high\n      treason by levying war, there must be an\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_81\">                                78</span>\n\n\n\n\n      insurrection, there must be force accompanying\n      that   insurrection;   and    it   must   be    for   the\n      accomplishment of an object of a general nature&quot;.\n</blockquote>\n\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_141\"><span class=\"citetext\" data-sentiment=\"Pos\" data-docid=\"1769219\" id=\"span_82\">76.          The Apex Court in the case of <a href=\"/doc/1769219/\" id=\"a_79\">STATE (NCT OF\n\nDELHI)   VS.    NAVJOT       SANDHU       ALIAS      AFSAN        GURU</a>\n\nreported in (2005)11 SCC 600 has discussed as to what\n\namounts to waging war or abetting or attempting to waging\n\nwar, punishable under <a href=\"/doc/786750/\" id=\"a_80\">Section121</a> IPC and has held as\n\nunder:</span>\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_49\">      258. In interpreting the expression &#x27;waging war&#x27;,\n      the Indian cases of pre- independence days,\n      though few they are, by and large cited with\n      approval the 18th and 19th century English\n      authorities. The term &#x27;wages war&#x27; was considered\n      to be a substitute for &#x27;levying war&#x27; in the English\n      Statute of High Treason of 1351 i.e Statute 25,\n      Edward III, c.2. In the famous book of Sir James\n      F. Stephen    &quot;A History of the Criminal Law of\n      England&quot; (1883 publication), it was noted that the\n      principal heads of treason as ascertained by that\n      Statute were: (1) &#x27;imagining&#x27; the King&#x27;s death&quot; (2)\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_83\">                           79</span>\n\n\n\n\nlevying war and (3) adhering to the King&#x27;s\nenemies.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_50\">264. Whether this exposition of law on the\nsubject of levying war continues to be relevant in\nthe present day and in the context of great socio-\n</blockquote><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_142\">political developments that have taken place is a\nmoot point.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_143\">272. Sections 121 and 121-A occur in the chapter\n&quot;Offences against the State&quot;. The public peace is\ndisturbed     and   the   normal      channels    of   the\nGovernment are disrupted by such offences\nwhich are aimed at subverting the authority of\nthe Government or paralyzing the constitutional\nmachinery.     The expression &quot;war&quot; preceded by\nthe verb &quot;wages&quot; admits of many shades of\nmeaning and defies a definition with exactitude.\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_144\">274. The       conspiracy      to     commit     offences\npunishable      under       Section     121      attracts\npunishment      under     Section     121A     and     the\nmaximum sentence could be imprisonment for life.\nThe other limb of Section 121A is the conspiracy\nto overawe by means of criminal force or the\nshow of criminal force, the Central Government or\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_84\">                              80</span>\n\n\n\n\nany State Government. The explanation to Section\n121-A clarifies that it is not necessary that any\nact or illegal omission should take place pursuant\nto the conspiracy, in order to constitute the said\noffence.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_145\">275. War, terrorism and violent acts to overawe\nthe established Government have many things in\ncommon. It is not too easy to distinguish them,\nbut one thing is certain, the concept of war\nimbedded in Section 121 is not to be understood\nin international law sense of inter-country war\ninvolving military operations by and between two\nor more hostile countries. Section 121 is not\nmeant to punish prisoners of war of a belligerent\nnation. Apart from the legislative history of the\nprovision      and    the    understanding   of   the\nexpression by various High Courts during the pre-\nindependence days, the Illustration to Section\n121 itself makes it clear that &#x27;war&#x27; contemplated\nby Section 121 is not conventional warfare\nbetween two nations. Organizing or joining an\ninsurrection against the Government of India is\nalso a form of war. &#x27;Insurrection&#x27; as defined in\ndictionaries    and     as    commonly   understood\nconnotes a violent uprising by a group directed\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_85\">                          81</span>\n\n\n\n\nagainst the Government in power or the civil\nauthorities. &quot;Rebellion, revolution and civil war\nare progressive stages in the development of civil\nunrest the most rudimentary form of which is\n&#x27;insurrection&#x27; vide Pan American World Air Inc. Vs.\nActna Cas &amp; Sur Co. [505, F.R. 2d, 989 at P.\n1017]. An act of insurgency is different from\nbelligerency.   It   needs    to   be   clarified   that\ninsurrection is only illustrative of the expression\n&#x27;war&#x27; and it is seen from the old English\nauthorities referred to supra that it would cover\nsituations analogous to insurrection if they tend\nto undermine the authority of the Ruler or\nGovernment.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_146\">276. Unlawful assemblies, riots, insurrections,\nrebellions, levying of war are offences which run\ninto each other and not capable of being marked\noff by perfectly definite boundaries. All of them\nhave in common one feature, namely, that the\nnormal tranquility of a civilized society is, in each\nof the cases mentioned, disturbed either by actual\nforce or at least by the show and threat of it.\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_147\">277. To this list has to be added &quot;terrorist acts&quot;\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_51\">which are so conspicuous now-a-days.           Though\nevery terrorist act does not amount to waging\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_86\">                         82</span>\n\n\n\n\nwar, certain terrorist acts can also constitute the\noffence of waging war and there is no dichotomy\nbetween the two.      Terrorist acts can manifest\nthemselves into acts of war.        Terrorist acts\nprompted by an intention to strike at the\nsovereign authority of the State/Government,\ntantamount to waging war irrespective of the\nnumber involved or the force employed.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_52\">282. The intention and purpose of the warlike\noperations directed against the governmental\nmachinery is an important criterion. If the object\nand purpose is to strike at the sovereign authority\nof the Ruler or the Government to achieve a public\nand general purpose in contradistinction to a\nprivate and a particular purpose, that is an\nimportant indicia of waging war. Of course, the\npurpose must be intended to be achieved by use\nof force and arms and by defiance of government\ntroops or armed personnel deployed to maintain\npublic tranquility.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_53\">283. However, a settled proposition is that there\nneed not be the pomp and pageantry usually\nassociated with war such as the offenders\nforming themselves in battle line and arraying in\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_87\">                          83</span>\n\n\n\n\na warlike manner. Even a stealthy operation to\noverwhelm    the     armed,   or   other   personnel\ndeployed by the Government and to attain a\ncommanding position by which terms could be\ndictated to the Government might very well be an\nact of waging war.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_54\">284. The court must be cautious in adopting an\napproach which has the effect of bringing within\nthe fold of Section 121 all acts of lawless and\nviolent acts resulting in destruction of public\nproperties, etc., and all acts of violent resistance\nto the armed personnel to achieve certain political\nobjectives. The moment it is found that the object\nsought to be attained is of a general public nature\nor has a political hue, the offensive violent acts\ntargeted against the armed forces and public\nofficials should not be branded as acts of waging\nwar. The expression &quot;waging war&quot; should not be\nstretched too far to hold that all the acts of\ndisrupting public order and peace irrespective of\ntheir magnitude and repercussions could be\nreckoned as acts of waging war against the\nGovernment. A balanced and realistic approach is\ncalled for in construing the expression &quot;waging\nwar&quot; irrespective of how it was viewed in the long\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_88\">                         84</span>\n\n\n\n\nlong past. An organized movement attended with\nviolence and attacks against the public officials\nand armed forces while agitating for the repeal of\nan unpopular law or for preventing burdensome\ntaxes were viewed as acts of treason in the form\nof levying war.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_55\">285. An aspect on which a clarification is called\nfor is in regard to the observation made in the old\ndecisions that &quot;neither the number engaged, nor\nthe force employed, nor the species of weapons\nwith which they may be armed&quot; is really material\nto prove the offence of levying/waging war.\nThese are not irrelevant factors.       They will\ncertainly help the court in forming an idea\nwhether the intention and design to wage war\nagainst the established Government exists or the\noffence falls short of it.     For instance, the\nfirepower or the devastating potential of the arms\nand explosives that may be carried by a group of\npersons-may be large or small, as in the present\ncase, and the scale of violence that follows may\nat times become useful indicators of the nature\nand dimension of the action resorted to. These,\ncoupled with the other factors, may give rise to an\ninference of waging war.\n</blockquote><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_89\">                                  85</span>\n\n\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_56\">    286. In order to give rise to the offence of waging\n    war, the avowed purpose and design of the\n    offence   need      not    be     to    substitute     another\n    authority for the Government of India. Even if the\n    conspired purpose and objective falls short of\n    installing some other authority or entity in the\n    place of an established Government, it does not\n    detract from the offence of waging war. There is\n    no warrant for such truncated interpretation. The\n    chances of success of such an operation need not\n    be assessed to judge the nature of criminality.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_57\">CONSPIRACY TO OVER-AWE (SECTION 121-A <a href=\"/doc/1569253/\" id=\"a_81\">IPC</a>)\n\n            121A. Conspiracy to commit offences\n\n      punishable by section 121\n\n      Whoever within or without India conspires to\n      commit any of the offences punishable by\n      section 121, or conspires to overawe, by means\n      of criminal force or the show of criminal force,\n      the     Central     Government            or   any     State\n      Government,             shall        be   punished      with\n      imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_90\">                                     86</span>\n\n\n\n\n             either description which may extend to ten\n             years, and shall also be liable to fine.\n                 Explanation- To constitute a conspiracy\n             under this section, it is not necessary that any\n             act or illegal omission shall make place in\n             pursuance thereof.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_58\">INGREDIENTS OF THE SECTION:\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_59\">77.              Section    121-A     deals    with     two     kinds   of\n\nconspiracies:\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_60\">      (i)        Conspiring     within   or   outside   India    to\n                 Commit any of the offences punishable by\n                 Section 121.I.P.C;\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_61\">      (ii)       Conspiring to overawe the Government by\n                 means of Criminal force or the show of\n                 criminal force.\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_148\">Hence the essential ingredient of the offence under this\n\nsection would be:\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_62\">      (i)        waging war against the Govt. of India; or\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_63\">      (ii)       attempting to wage war against the Govt.\n</blockquote><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_149\">                 of India; or\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_91\">                                 87</span>\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_150\">      (iii)   abetting the waging war against the Govt.\n</p><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_151\">              of India.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_152\">      (iv)    Conspire to overawe by means of criminal\n              force or the show of criminal force.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_153\">\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_154\"><span class=\"citetext\" data-sentiment=\"Pos\" data-docid=\"1416180\" id=\"span_92\">78.           A conspiracy is a combination of two or more\n\npersons to do an unlawful act, or to do a lawful act by\n\nunlawful means. This section draws a distinction between\n\nthe Government of India and State Government. Any\n\nconspiracy to change the form of the Government of India or\n\nany State Government, even though it may amount to an\n\noffence under another section of the Code, would not be an\n\noffence under this section, unless it is a conspiracy to\n\noverawe such Government by means of criminal force or\n\nshow of criminal force, as was illustrated in the matter of\n\n<a href=\"/doc/1416180/\" id=\"a_82\">Jhabwala v Emperor</a> (1933) 55 All 1040.</span>\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_155\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_156\">      The word &#x27;overawe&#x27; clearly imports more than the\n\ncreation of apprehension or alarm or even perhaps fears. The\n\nphrase &quot;conspiracy to overawe&quot; has been used in this\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_93\">                              88</span>\n\n\n\n\nprovision of the <a href=\"/doc/1569253/\" id=\"a_83\">IPC</a>. Overawe in lay man&#x27;s terms means to\n\nsubdue, frighten or intimidate. The words &#x27;conspires to\n\noverawe by means of criminal force or the show of criminal\n\nforce, the Government of India, or any State Government&#x27; in\n\nthis section clearly embrace not merely a conspiracy to raise\n\na general insurrection, but also a conspiracy to overawe the\n\nGovernment of India or any State Government by the\n\norganization of a serious riot or a large and tumultuous\n\nunlawful assembly as was seen in the case of Ramanand v.\n\nEmperor, (1950) 30 Pat 152.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_157\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"RespArg\" id=\"p_158\">79.         It appears to connote the creation of a situation\n\nin which the members of the Central or the State\n\nGovernment feel themselves compelled to choose between\n\nyielding to force or exposing themselves or members of the\n\npublic to a very serious danger. It is not necessary that the\n\ndanger should be a danger of assassination or of bodily\n\ninjury to themselves. The danger might well be a danger to\n\npublic property or to the safety of members of the general\n\npublic. (Ramanand vs. Emperor, (1950) 30 Pat 152).\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_94\">                                89</span>\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"RespArg\" id=\"p_159\">80.            A slogan that Government can be changed by an\n\narmed revolution does not mean that there is a conspiracy to\n\nchange the Government by criminal force. At best it means\n\nthat the petitioners want to educate the people that by force\n\nonly the Government could be changed (Arvindan, v State\n\nof Kerala, 1983 CrLJ 1259).\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_160\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_161\">81.            The explanation to Section 121-A states that to\n\nconstitute a conspiracy under this Section, it is not\n\nnecessary that any act or illegal omission shall take place in\n\npursuance thereof. The words in the section clearly embrace\n\nnot merelely a conspiracy to raise a general insurrection, but\n\nalso a conspiracy to overawe the Government of India or any\n\nState Government by the organization of a serious riot or a\n\nlarge and tumultuous unlawful assembly.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_162\">\nSEDITION (SECTION 124A <a href=\"/doc/1569253/\" id=\"a_84\">IPC</a>)\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_163\">82.            Yet another offence alleged against the accused\n\nis Sedition.    <a href=\"/doc/1641007/\" id=\"a_85\">Section 124A</a> of the Indian Penal Code deals\n\nwith `Sedition&#x27; which reads as under:\n</p><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_95\">                                 90</span><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_164\">\n\n\n\n\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_64\">124A. Sedition.--Whoever, by words, either\nspoken or written, or by signs, or by visible\nrepresentation, or otherwise, brings or attempts to\nbring into hatred or contempt, or excites or\nattempts     to    excite    disaffection      towards, the\nGovernment established by law in India, shall be\npunished with imprisonment for life, to which fine\nmay be added, or with imprisonment which may\nextend to three years, to which fine may be\nadded, or with fine.\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_65\">Explanation 1.--The expression &quot;disaffection&quot;\nincludes disloyalty and all feelings of enmity.\n</blockquote><pre id=\"pre_3\">Explanation            2.--       Comments         expressing\ndisapprobation         of    the       measures     of    the\n</pre><blockquote id=\"blockquote_66\">Government with a view to obtain their alteration\nby lawful means, without exciting or attempting\nto excite hatred, contempt or disaffection, do not\nconstitute an offence under this section.\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_67\">Explanation            3.--       Comments         expressing\ndisapprobation of the administrative or other\naction of the Government without exciting or\nattempting        to   excite        hatred,   contempt    or\ndisaffection, do not constitute an offence under\nthis section.]\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_96\">                                  91</span>\n\n\n\n\n</blockquote><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_165\">83.           The Apex Court in the case of NAZIR KHAN AND\n\nOTHERS V/S. STATE OF DELHI reported in (2003)8 SCC\n\n461 at para 37 has held as under:\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_166\">\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_68\">      Section 124-A deals with &#x27;Sedition&#x27;. Sedition is a\n      crime against society nearly allied to that of\n      treason, and it frequently precedes treason by a\n      short     interval.    Sedition    in      itself   is   a\n      comprehensive term, and it embraces all those\n      practices, whether by word, deed, or writing,\n      which are calculated to disturb the tranquillity of\n      the     State,   and   lead     ignorant     persons     to\n      endeavour to subvert the Government and laws of\n      the country. The objects of sedition generally are\n      to induce discontent and insurrection, and stir up\n      opposition to the Government, and bring the\n      administration of justice into contempt; and the\n      very tendency of sedition is to incite the people to\n      insurrection and rebellion. &quot;Sedition has been\n      described as disloyalty in action, and the law\n      considers as sedition all those practices which\n      have for their object to excite discontent or\n      dissatisfaction, to create public disturbance, or to\n      lead to civil war; to bring into hatred or contempt\n      the Sovereign or the Government, the laws or\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_97\">                              92</span>\n\n\n\n\n     constitutions of the realm, and generally all\n     endeavours to promote public disorder.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_69\">PROMOTING ENMITY BETWEEN DIFFERENT GROUPS\n(SECTION 153-A <a href=\"/doc/1569253/\" id=\"a_86\">IPC</a>)\n\n     153A. Promoting enmity between different\n     groups on grounds of religion, race, place of\n     birth, residence, language, etc., and doing\n     acts     prejudicial    to      maintenance     of\n     harmony.-(1) whoever-\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_70\">            (a)   by words, either spoken or written,\n                  or   by    signs    or    by   visible\n                  representations      or    otherwise,\n                  promotes or attempts to promote, on\n                  grounds of religion, race, place of\n                  birth, residence, language, caste or\n                  community or any other ground\n                  whatsoever, disharmony or feelings\n                  of enmity, hatred or ill-will between\n                  different religious, racial, language\n                  or regional groups or castes or\n                  communities, or\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_98\">                        93</span>\n\n\n\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_71\">(b)   commits any act which is prejudicial\n      to the       maintenance of            harmony\n      between different religious, racial,\n      language         or   regional       groups     or\n      castes or communities, and which\n      disturbs or is likely to disturb the\n      public tranquillity, [or]\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_72\">(c)   organizes any exercise, movement,\n      drill   or        other      similar      activity\n      intending that the participants in\n      such activity shall use or be trained\n      to use criminal force or violence or\n      knowing it to be likely that the\n      participants in such activity will use\n      or be trained to use criminal force or\n      violence, or participates in such\n      activity        intending     to    use   or    be\n      trained to use criminal force or\n      violence or knowing it to be likely\n      that the participants in such activity\n      will use or be trained to use criminal\n      force      or     violence,        against     any\n      religious,         racial,     language         or\n      regional          group       or     caste      or\n      community and such activity for any\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_99\">                               94</span>\n\n\n\n\n                   reason whatsoever causes or is\n                   likely to cause fear or alarm or a\n                   feeling   of    insecurity     amongst\n                   members of such religious, racial,\n                   language or regional group or caste\n                   or community,\n\n           shall   be   punished    with     imprisonment\n           which may extend to three years, or with\n           fine, or with both.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_73\">           Offence      committed       in      place   of\n           worship, etc.,- (2) Whoever commits an\n           offence specified in sub-section (1) in any\n           place of worship or in any assembly\n           engaged in the performance of religious\n           worship or religious ceremonies, shall be\n           punished with imprisonment which may\n           extend to five years and shall also be\n           liable to fine.\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_167\">84.        Promoting enemity between different groups on\n\ngrounds of religion, the Legislature contemplates that the\n\nwords spoken or written which attempts to promote hatred,\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_100\">                                 95</span>\n\n\n\n\netc., would create sufficient mischief so as to fall within the\n\nscope of the section 153-A.\n\n\n\nOTHER OFFENCE UNDER IPC\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_168\">85.         <a href=\"/doc/305995/\" id=\"a_87\">Section        295</a>         IPC        reads     as\n\nunder:\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_169\">\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_74\">               295.       Injuring or defiling place\n         of worship with intent to insult the\n         religion of any class.- Whoever destroys,\n         damages or defiles any place of worship, or\n         any object held sacred by any class of\n         persons   with   the    intention of   thereby\n         insulting the religion of any class of\n         persons or with the knowledge that any\n         class of persons is likely to consider such\n         destruction, damage or defilement as an\n         insult to their religion, shall be punishable\n         with imprisonment of either description for\n         a term which may extend to two years, or\n         with fine, or with both.\n</blockquote><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_101\">                                   96</span>\n\n\n\n\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_170\">86.         <a href=\"/doc/222396/\" id=\"a_88\">Section 427</a> of the Indian Penal Code reads as\n\nunder:\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_171\">\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_75\">      427. Mischief         causing       damage     to   the\n      amount of fifty rupees.- Whoever commits\n      mischief and thereby causes loss or damage to\n      the amount of fifty rupees or upwards, shall be\n      punished with imprisonment of either description\n      for a term which may extend to two years, or with\n      fine, or with both.\n</blockquote>\n\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_172\">87.         <a href=\"/doc/1517318/\" id=\"a_89\">Section 436</a> of the Indian Penal Code reads as\n\nunder:\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_173\">\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_76\">      436. Mischief by fire or explosive substance\n      with intent to destroy house, etc.,- Whoever\n      commits    mischief    by    fire   or   any   explosive\n      substance, intending to cause, or knowing it to be\n      likely that he will thereby cause, the destruction\n      of any building which is ordinarily used as a\n      place of worship or as a human dwelling or as a\n      place for the custody of property, shall be\n      punished with [imprisonment for life], or with\n      imprisonment of either description for a term\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_102\">                              97</span>\n\n\n\n\n    which may extend to ten years, and shall also be\n    liable to fine.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_174\">OFFENCES UNDER THE EXPLOSIVES ACT, 1884:\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_77\">           &quot;SECTION-9B. Punishment for certain\n           offences.- (1) Whoever, in contravention of\n           rules made under <a href=\"/doc/1335368/\" id=\"a_90\">section 5</a> or of the\n           conditions of a licence granted under the\n           said rules-\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_78\">           (a)    manufactures, imports or exports any\n                  explosive shall be punishable with\n                  imprisonment for a term which may\n                  extend to three years, or with fine\n                  which may extend to five thousand\n                  rupees, or with both;\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_79\">           (b)    possesses, uses, sells or transports\n                  any explosive shall be punishable\n                  with imprisonment for a term which\n                  may extend to two years or with fine\n                  which may extend to three thousand\n                  rupees or with both: and\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_80\">           (c)    in any other case, with fine which\n                  may extend to one thousand rupees.\n</blockquote><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_103\">                            98</span>\n\n\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_81\">       (2)     Whoever     in       contravention   of   a\nnotification    issued        U/s     6    manufactures,\npossesses or imports any explosive shall be\npunishable with imprisonment for a term which\nmay extend to three years or with fine which may\nextend to five thousand rupees or with both; and\nin the case of importation by water, the owner\nand master of the vessel or in the case of\nimportation by air, the owner and the master of\nthe aircraft, in which the explosive is imported\nshall, in the absence of reasonable excuse, each\nbe punishable with fine which may extend to five\nthousand rupees.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_82\">(3)    Whoever,-\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_83\"> (a)   manufactures, sells, transports, imports,\n       exports or possesses any explosive in\n       contravention of the provisions of clause (a)\n       of section 6A; or\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_84\">(b)    sells, delivers or despatches any explosive\n       in contravention of the provisions of clause\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_85\">       (b) of that section,\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_104\">                                        99</span>\n\n\n\n\n      Shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term\n      which may extend to three years or with fine or\n      with both, or\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_86\">      (c)         in contravention of the provisions of section\n                  8 fails to give notice of any accident shall\n                  be punishable,-\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_87\">                  (i)       with fine which may extend to five\n                            hundred rupees, or\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_88\">                  (ii)      if the accident is attended by loss of\n                            human life, with imprisonment for a\n                            term which may extend to three\n                            months or with fine or with both.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_89\">88.               <a href=\"/doc/1056565/\" id=\"a_91\">Section 3</a> of the Explosive Substance Act, 1908\n\nreads as under:\n</blockquote>\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_90\">       3.    Punishment for causing explosion likely\n       to endanger life or property.- Any person who\n       unlawfully and maliciously causes by-\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_91\">            (a)          any explosive substance an explosion of\n                         a nature likely to endanger life or to\n                         cause serious injury to property shall,\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_105\">                                          100</span>\n\n\n\n\n                    whether any injury to person or property\n                    has been actually caused or not, be\n                    punished with imprisonment for life, or\n                    with rigorous imprisonment of either\n                    description which shall not be less than\n                    ten years, and shall also be liable to\n                    fine;\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_92\">         (b)        any          special       category        explosive\n                    substance an explosion of a nature\n                    likely to endanger life or to cause\n                    serious injury to property shall, whether\n                    any injury to person or property has\n                    been         actually      caused     or   not,   be\n                    punished         with      death,     or    rigorous\n                    imprisonment for life, and shall also be\n                    liable to fine.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_175\">89.            <a href=\"/doc/895566/\" id=\"a_92\">Section 4</a> of the Explosive Substance Act, 1908\n\nreads as under:\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_93\">               4.     Punishment for attempt to cause\n      explosion,            or     for      making      or     keeping\n      explosive with intent to endanger life or\n      property- Any person                     who unlawfully and\n      maliciously-\n</blockquote><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_106\">                            101</span>\n\n\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_94\">  (a)     does any act with intent to cause by an\n          explosive substance or special category\n          explosive substance, or conspires to\n          cause by an explosive substance or\n          special category explosive substance, an\n          explosion of a nature likely to endanger\n          life   or   to   cause   serious    injury   to\n          property; or\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_95\">  (b)     makes or has in his possession or under\n          his control any explosive substance or\n          special category explosive substance\n          with    intent    by     means     thereof   to\n          endanger life, or cause serious injury to\n          property, or to enable any other person\n          by means thereof to endanger life or\n          cause serious injury to property in India,\n\n        Shall, whether any explosion does or does\nnot take place and whether any injury to person\nor property has been actually caused or not, be\npunished,-\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_96\">          (i)     in the case of any explosive\n                  substance, with imprisonment for\n                  life, or with imprisonment of either\n                  description for a term which may\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_107\">                               102</span>\n\n\n\n\n                      extend to ten years, and shall\n                      also be liable to fine;\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_97\">               (ii)   in case of any special category\n                      explosive substance, with rigorous\n                      imprisonment for life, or with\n                      rigorous imprisonment for a term\n                      which may extend to ten years,\n                      and shall also be liable to fine.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_98\">90.         <a href=\"/doc/1335368/\" id=\"a_93\">Section 5</a> of the Explosive Substance Act, 1908\n\nreads as under:\n</blockquote>\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_99\">      5.   Punishment for making or possessing\n           explosives          under            suspicious\n           circumstances.- Any person who makes or\n           knowingly has in his possession or under\n           his control any explosive substance or\n           special category explosive substance, under\n           such circumstances as to give rise to a\n           reasonable suspicion that he is not making it\n           or does not have it in his possession or\n           under his control for a lawful object, shall,\n           unless he can show that he made it or had it\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_108\">                                  103</span>\n\n\n\n\n           in his possession or under his control for a\n           lawful object, be punished, -\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_100\">            (a) in the case of any explosive substance,\n               with imprisonment for a term which\n               may extend to ten years, and shall also\n               be liable to fine;</blockquote>\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_101\">            (b) in the case of any special category\n               explosive    substance,     with   rigorous\n               imprisonment for life, or with rigorous\n               imprisonment for a term which may\n               extend to ten years, and shall also be\n               liable to fine.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_176\">91.         Rule 5 of the Explosive Rules, 1983 reads as\n\nunder:\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_177\">\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_102\">      5.    Prohibition             of     unauthorized\n      explosives.- No. person shall import, export,\n      transport, manufacture, possess, use or sell any\n      explosive which is not an authorized explosive.\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"RespArg\" id=\"p_178\">            Provided that nothing in this rule shall\n      apply to the manufacture and possession for test\n      and trial purposes and not for sale of a new\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_109\">                                104</span>\n\n\n\n\n      explosive composition under development at a\n      place specially approved for the purpose by the\n      Chief Controller in a licensed factory.&quot;\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_179\">\n\nMENSREA\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_180\">92.            In the background of this legal position, before\n\nwe proceed to appreciate the evidence and arguments, it is\n\nnecessary to find out what is the guilty mind behind this\n\nserial blast. In other words, mensrea, i.e., the idealogy that\n\nbrought all these accused together to commit the acts for\n\nwhich they were prosecuted and the trial Court has found\n\nthem guilty.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_181\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_182\">93.            In the course of investigation, the police have\n\nseized several incriminating materials in including books,\n\nliterature and pamphlets from the possession of several of\n\nthese accused. A perusal of the same gives an insight into\n\nthe activities of these accused and the ideology which\n\nprompted them to indulge in these illegal activities.\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_110\">                               105</span>\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_183\">94.         Late Hazarath Siddique has written a book by\n\nname &quot;Zamamul Jihad&quot; in Persian language and it was\n\ntranslated into English language. Late Hazarath Siddique, in\n\nthat book has given an ultimatum of religious war as under:\n\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_103\">            &quot;May peace to be on you,\n\n            We, the muslims of the world, are aware of\n      and observing that Islam is in the grips of Kafirs,\n      in the condition of orphanage, poverty, powerless\n      and without any supporter, The oppression and\n      the injustice of the Kafirs has increased beyond\n      its limits. The decision of the Holy Quran was\n      that the power of issuing orders for good deeds\n      and prohibition from bad deeds, should be only in\n      the   hands    of   Muslims.   This    dignity   has\n      completely gone from our hands into the hands of\n      Kafers. In this condition there is in the rise of the\n      calamity of Dooms-day. We are seeing what of\n      Arabs and what of Non-Arabs, every muslim is\n      involved in the worst condition of degradation. In\n      this worthless age, the nominated Khilafath has\n      been thrown off. I can dare say with my spiritual\n      insight that there is no patron for the muslims in\n      the present time. Every nation and tribe of the\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_111\">                        106</span>\n\n\n\n\nmuslims has confused and has been scattered\naway like the flying rubbish grass.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_104\">Oh; The Heroes of Islam, We should not be\ndesparated in this situation as our Nabi-e-Kareem\n[May prevail his religion] is the fountain-head of\nthe prophets. Our religion is the perfect religion\nand our Holy book is the complete book. Our Holy\nQuran has certified us that we are the leaders of\nhumanity. We are fully sure of the prophecy of\nour Holy prophet [may prevail his religion] i.e.,\n&quot;The Resurrection of Humanity will take place\nunder my feet&quot;. The banner of the Exalration of\nAllah will upheld only by us. According to the\nsayings of our Holy Prophet [May prevail his\nreligion i.e., All the prophets will be under my\nbanner on that day&quot;. Every nation will take\nprotection under the banner of Islam. In virtue of\nthis proof, the version of the Holy Quran is &quot;We\nwill raise you as the head of all those prophets.\nFortunately the nations of the prophets are\ngathered together in India by divine power. If we\nmake them to embrace Islam, the above prophecy\nwill be fulfilled. In this behalf, I do not say by\nmyself, but I have received continuous divine\ngood news. On the basis of these good news, I\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_112\">                        107</span>\n\n\n\n\nhave started the Movement of the Propagation of\nIslam since 20 years. I have got revelations that\nthe whole of India will embrace Islam. The Kafirs\nof India understanding that this movement is\nuntolerable have spent their level best of their\ncheating and treachery for its uprootal. But we\nhave tried our level best to make it successful. by\nour sincere determination. In many place, our\nspeeches have been prohibited and we were sent\nto jails. We were arrested and released by\nsecurity and penalty bonds in several places. We\nwere dragged in bazaars with chains for the\ncause    of   this   movement.     Under     these\nundescribable circumstances, the propagation of\nIslam has been stopped. We were put to troubles,\nso we have to flight to the Independent Territories\naccording to the Hadith for the organization of\nIslamic powers to declare the Religious war. We\ncan make India to embrace Islam only by this\nsource. According to version of the Holy Quran,\nwe have to remove the obstacles that are coming\nin the way of the propagation of Islam.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_105\">&quot;May be the glory to Allah. Our determination\nhave brought the clear result, i.e., within a few\nmonths of our attempts, the Heroes of the\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_113\">                         108</span>\n\n\n\n\nIndependent     Territories    came    forward    with\nenthusiasm for our works and according to the\nversion   of   the   Holy     Quran,   they   pledged\nthemselves in my hands believing in me as the\nHead of the Religious war.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_106\">&quot;Oh: the Heroes of Islam&quot;, may praise be Allah,\naccording to my vision, Ghazi Abdus Subhan\nKhan alias Shehe-Jehan, the Nawab of Khar,\npledged    himself    with      sincere   faith   and\nknowledge. Afterwards according to my vision,\nAllama Amiruddin Khan Saheb Dabar alias\nMoulvi Shanger Gull, has also pledged himself in\nmy hands. This sincere and foresight man wrote\nletters to moulvis, Nawabs, Malaks and Khans of\nIndependent territories advising them to pledge\nthemselves in my hands as he has done. This\nnoble man has expressed in letters addressed to\nthem that he was expecting a man who is\ncapable of exploiting the propagation of Islam\nwith his purity and in according to the principles\nof the Holy Quran and his expectation has\nfulfilled by my personality. This promised noble\nman has submitted to me a letter of confession\nstating that he will start on my order&#x27;s whenever\ngiven to them with numberless armies as flood of\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_114\">                          109</span>\n\n\n\n\nwater for Jihad. I have received another letter\nwith a bond of pledge from Moulvi Nawab Meeran\nJan alias Sayyal. He states that &quot;I have met the\ndeputation of Deendar Anjuman whom I found to\nbe very faithfull to Islam and I felt glad after\nunderstanding the aims and objects of your\nAnjuman which are quiet consistent with the\nprinciples of Nabi-a-Kareem [May prevail his\nreligion]. The past spiritual emotion will be born in\nmuslims at the expected occasions. The muslims\nin general and especially the heroes of the\nindependent     territories   are   very   glad   and\nsatisfied   with      these    goodness.    Therefore\nconsidering my religious duty, I am ready to do\nall necessary sacrifices in the service of Islam as\nit is true devotee.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_107\">&quot;Oh; Heroes of Islam&quot; according to the version of\nthe Holy Quran Kareem. The British Government,\nand the Rajas of India have become enraged and\nfurious with the organization and discipline of our\nmovement. Ten years, ago when I was in\nYagisthan the British Government from fear of\ntheir destruction they arrested me by their deceipt\nand treachery and I have been confined in the\ncity limits of Hyderabad Dn. in order to put an\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_115\">                           110</span>\n\n\n\n\nend to our movement. The Kafirs do not know\nthat the plant which is flourishing by the Divine\nhands will not be fruitless.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_108\">&quot;Be the Glory to Allah&quot;. The Lords of the worlds\nhas maintained our movement by his own hands.\nIn such short period 40 spiritual men has been\nraised according to Hadis. As return of Muslims\nfrom      Hudebeya      become     the     cause      of\nstrengthening of     Islam, so our return from\nJagistan on security also become strengthing of\nIslam. In this year, I sent a deputation to the\nIndependent Territories in the leadership of Ghazi\nMahmoodul-Qureshi, the Commander Islam to\nawaken the Muslim Heroes. The organization has\nbeen   compelled     by    these   faithfull    Muslim\ndevotees. My object has been obtained. Again an\nanother    deputation     has   been     sent   in   the\nleadership of Moizzul Millath, Habbibullah Sahab\nfor the organization of the ruler of Afghan Nation.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_109\">On the way at Chukderra, the British Govet.\narrested me numbers of the deputation and sent\nback on security bonds. By this action it appears\nto be that the British Govt. do not like that the\nMuslim should be awakened and exhibit the\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_116\">                        111</span>\n\n\n\n\nmerits of the Islam in the religious war for\ncompelling the Kafirs to embrace Islam. The Govt.\nwants that the footings of the Kafirs should be\nfirm and Islam should be disgraceful among them\nand to be destroyed and the Muslims should be\ndeprived of the dignity of issuing orders for good\nneeds and prohibiting from bad deeds for ever\nand the disgrace and dishonour of Our Nabi-e-\nKareem [May prevail his religion] should also be\ncontinued permanently.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_110\">&quot;Oh; the lovers and sacrifiers of Islam, now the\nBritish Govt. prevents the revival of Islam as the\nGovt. of Iran had done, in its early period. The\nMuslims Heroes of early period wanted that the\nwhole of Iran should embrace Islam and we want\nalso that the whole of India should embrace\nIslam. In that enlightened period according to the\nversion of the Holy Quran, they upheld the\nbanner of religious war and put an end to the\nobstacles that were coming in the way of Islam.\nIn the same manner, in this dark period Allah\nwants to enforce the Kafirs of India to embrace\nIslam by means of religious war. Now, according\nto the version of the Holy Quran and Hadis, I\norder you to give an Ultimatum as to begin the\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_117\">                        112</span>\n\n\n\n\nreligious war with the British Govt. until they\nshould embrace Islam and support us in our\nworks. I have sent this order of religious war by\nGhazi Mahmoodul-urashi, the Commander of\nIslamic force. As soon as you receive this order,\nall of you who have pledged yourselves in my\nhands should assemble in one place and elect an\nAmir. Afterwards you must complete the rulers of\nSwad, Deer, Chitral and Afghanistan by all\nnecessary means to co-operate with you all their\narms   and    ammunitions.    After   a   complete\npreparation, a deputation should be sent to the\nViceroy of India for their embracement of Islam\nand the problem of India embracing Islam should\nbe put forth before them and we should say that\nwe have no connection with the worldly affairs\nand we do not want that the British Nation\nshould not be our rulers, and also we do no find\nany other nation so enlightened, wise and\ncapable rulers of India except the British Nation.\nWhat can we do? The Obstacle come in the way\nof the propagation of Islam compels to come\nagainst the British Govt. Now in such a case, we\nwant that the British Nation should embrace\nIslam and make an announcement of their\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_118\">                          113</span>\n\n\n\n\nembracement from the throne. If it is undesirable\non their part, they should hand over the powers\nto us so that we can easily Islamise India means\nof Jihad. In case the Government do not accept it,\nthe &#x27;AMIR&#x27; of the deputation should obtain their\nrefusal in writing, so that it may be proof at the\ntime of the execution. Thereafter they may come\nback quietly after giving a writing &#x27;ULTIMATUM&#x27;\nin this behalf to the Govt. declaring that we have\ngiven suggestion to the Government, stating that\nthe settlement should be in the favour of the\nIslam without war. They did not consent to our\nsuggestion. Hence we are bound to declare the\nreligious war to India. In behalf of this, there is an\norder in Holy Quran to fight with the neighbouring\nKafirs. According to the version of the Quran, we\nare bound to do the religious war in India until all\nthe obstacles that are coming in the way of the\npropagation    of   Islam   should   be   completely\nremoved off.\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_111\">&#x27;OH HEROES OF ISLAM&#x27;, There is promise of\nAllah in the Holy Quran, that VICTORY is in our\nfavour. Try your best.\n</blockquote><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_119\">                            114</span>\n\n\n\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_112\">      D/18th   Shawal   1364   [Sd.]   Siddiq   Deendar\n      Chanbaswaishwer.&quot;\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_184\">95.        A photograph which is taken from the book\n\n&quot;Zamamul Jihad&quot; clearly demonstrates the intention which\n\nis behind the founder of the Deendar Anjuman Ashram.\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_120\">                              115</span>\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_185\">96.         The book titled &quot;World Teacher&quot;, Jagad Guru\n\nSarwar-E-alam (PBUH) is marked as Ex.P.132.             In the\n\npreface written by Siddiq Deendar Chanbasweshwar, it is\n\nmentioned that in the present times various persons have\n\nproclaimed themselves as Jagadgurus and their number\n\n(which increases with passage of time) itself indicates their\n\nfalsity. Allah has given me the opportunity and command to\n\nrectify this evil. Then he has mentioned that he travelled all\n\nover Karnataka and Mysore States for the cause of Islamic\n\npropagation. In the process he encountered so many &#x27;Jagad\n\nGurus and Naraharis&#x27; and was astonished to notice so many\n\nclaimants to the status of Jagad Guru. When the world is\n\none and the creator also is one, then how come there will be\n\nso many Jagad Gurus or World Teachers? There are many\n\nJagad Gurus amongst Hindus themselves.           Not a single\n\nperson has assumed the title of Jagad Guru (Sarwar-e-Alam)\n\namongst muslims because the muslims are aware of this\n\nterm Jagad Guru&#x27;s meaning and status.         They know fully\n\naware   that   no   other   person   except   Prophet   Hazrat\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_121\">                              116</span>\n\n\n\n\nmohammed (Pbuh) can caliberate the criteria and eligible to\n\nbe entitled as Jagad Guru.    When he commenced to write\n\nthis book in 1926, Prophet Mohammad (Pbuh) revealed\n\nhimself in his trance and commanded him to write his\n\nbiography.    Therefore, he has fully projected the holy\n\npersonality of Hazrat Mohammad (Phuh) in the book which\n\nis regarded by muslims as Sarwar-E-Alam.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_186\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_187\">97.          Further he discusses in the book that Prophet\n\nMomammad (Pbuh) alone can be the Jagad Guru (world\n\nteacher) whose advent is professed with particular signs and\n\nsymbols by all the foregone prophets. Then he has referred\n\nto the prophecies in the books of Persians, prophecy in\n\nPSALMS, prophesy in the book of Moses, Bibi Sarah&#x27;s\n\nProphecy, Prophecy in Gospel, Augury made by Egyptian\n\nScholars, Socrates Augury, Augury in China, Prophecy in\n\nRome, Prophecy in Spain, Persians. Then he has referred to\n\nProphecy in Bhagavat Puran, Kalki Puran and Bhavishyokt\n\nPuran.   He has also referred to the Prophecies about the\n\nadvent of Hazrat Mohammad (PBUH) in Vedas.         Then he\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_122\">                             117</span>\n\n\n\n\nproceeds to record that the Gurus are mentors of various\n\nmonasteries in the famous religious centres in India such as\n\nShringeri, Rameshwar, Chitradurga, Srirangam, Srihatti\n\nMath, Tintini, Warui, Hubli, Arud Math, Kadgamchi Math\n\netc., accepted the Islam after the manifestation of Prophet\n\nMohammed. Then he has narrated that the aphorisms of\n\nTulsidas to make it that even he believed in Prophet\n\nMohammad (Pbuh) in accordance as athe awaited and\n\nprophesied personality mentioned in Vedas and Puranas.\n\nTherefore the prophecy made by Eashwar to his beloved son\n\n&quot;Shanmukh&quot; regarding forthcoming religion and the future\n\nprophet is explained by Tulsidar in a couplet, &quot;Jab Sangram\n\nKa din hove - Bina Mohammad - (Pbuh) Par na hove&quot;\n\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_113\">(Sangram Puran) Translated to English it means that, &quot;When\n\na religion which makes Jehad obligatory and declares\n\nbounties of war legal, is come, none could achieve Salvation\n\nwithout acceptance &amp; belief in Prophet Mohammad (Pbuh)&quot;.\n</blockquote>\n\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_188\">98.         Then he refers to Basweshwar - the leader of the\n\nSaints of Deccan and Chanabasweshwar - another great\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_123\">                                118</span>\n\n\n\n\nSaint of Deccan (nephew of Basweshwar).       There is also a\n\nreference to Manak Prabhu in Humnabad.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_189\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_190\">99.          He has also referred to Chiristian community.\n\nThey believe Prophet Jesus Christ is the &#x27;Son of God&#x27;.\n\nChristians also believe that Allah has sent his servants as\n\nmessengers before Christ since they failed to accomplish the\n\ntask, he sent his beloved son. Even he could not succeed.\n\nTherefore, the spiritual father would himself descend down\n\nto this earth.   True to   say - Christians are awaiting and\n\nhave belief in Prophet Mohamad (Pbuh) prior to his birth.\n\nHowever, every Christian would thus definitely believe and\n\nrevere the    spiritual father of Jesus Christ (A.S) but is\n\nreluctant to declare it due to malice and hatred against\n\nmuslims.     The   spiritual   father   is none   but   Prophet\n\nMohammad (Pbuh).       In this respect, Prophet Mohammad\n\n(Pbuh) is present in the sacred places and each and every\n\nhouse of Christians. However, he states it is obligatory on\n\nmuslims to strive in the cause of Islam and establish peace\n\nand tranquility in the entire world.     The hearts and souls\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_124\">                              119</span>\n\n\n\n\ndevoid of this thought cannot claim to be true muslims. To\n\nabsorb other nations into Islamic fold is a distinct feature of\n\nIslam. May Allah give guidance to all muslims to discharge\n\nthis duty.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_191\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_192\">100.         Thus, he has set out 33 criterias of Jagad\n\nGurus.   These criterias were published in a poster on the\n\noccasion of International Religious Conference held at Asif\n\nNagar, Hyderabad, Deccan on 9th to 11th January 1935. He\n\ndeclared that Prophet Hazrath Mohammad alone can be the\n\nJagad Guru who is a perfect model to the four religions i.e.,\n\nBrahmin (Scholar), Kshatriya (Warrior), Vaishya (Trader) and\n\nShudra (Servant) based on human nature.           Further, he\n\nstates in all the religions besides various rituals, one\n\nimportant obligation is &#x27;animal sacrifice&#x27; which he has\n\ndiscussed in the book at length.     He states Brahmin who\n\nrefrained to consume flesh, have been enjoying performing\n\nYagna (animal sacrifice) as a foremost fundamental. Many\n\nperplexities have surfaced during those days concerning this\n\nissue.   In these circumstances, it is our prime duty to\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_125\">                              120</span>\n\n\n\n\ndiscern the Hindu community to be cognizant of their\n\nfundamentals. God willing, this article would do a wonderful\n\njob to establish Hindu-Muslim unity.        He recognized by\n\nexperience the sacrifice of cow as a main issue of\n\nestrangement. There are two reasons to perpetuate enmity.\n\nFirstly, ever since muslims arrived in India the hindu\n\npolitical leaders inculcated the creed of cow sanctity in\n\nHindu psyche. This flimsy creed was prevalent even before\n\namong rare communities without any basis.         When it was\n\nrealized that this issue has a potential to array an anti\n\nIslamic group, they began to give it leverage. And in this\n\nway they aroused antimuslim sentiments amongst their\n\nnation. When the moghal empire got weakened they made it\n\na main tool to achieve success. Then he declares, it is the\n\nduty of the muslims to disseminate the Hindus by all\n\npossible means that cow sacrifice is a fundamental of their\n\nreligion and consuming beef is a religious privilege. He want\n\nthem to repeatedly present the following facts:\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_126\">                                121</span>\n\n\n\n\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_114\">              &quot;Oh Hindu Bretheren, sacrifice the cow for\n        the sake of Hindu - Muslim unity. Sacrifice the\n        cow just as Walmiki had sacrificed at the\n        occasion of feast hosted in honour of Rishis and\n        Saints, or sacrifice the cow in the manner as\n        Manuji for the cause of harmony had sacrified 5\n        lakh cows at river Narbada. Sacrifice in the same\n        way as Sitaji had sacrificed one thousand cows\n        at the banks of river Ganges for protection from\n        natural calamities. Sacrifice in the same manner\n        as Krishnaji at the occasion of Yag (Animal\n        sacrifice) had sacrificed the cow.     Sacrifice the\n        cow just as Prophet Abraham (A.S) to serve his\n        guests had sacrificed calf. Sacrifice in the same\n        cause as Prophet Moses (A.S) to abolish idolatory\n        had sacrificed the cow. Sacrifice in the same way\n        as Prophet Hazrath Mohammad (Pbuh) to serve\n        his friends had sacrificed a cow.\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"PetArg\" id=\"p_193\">101.          Muslims can never renunciate the example set\n\nby the Prophet (Pbuh) pertaining cow sacrifice, even though\n\nother     nations    with    changing       circumstances      have\n\nrelinquished and reverted the example set by their Avtars\n\nand Sages.\n</p><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_127\">                             122</span><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_194\">\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"RespArg\" id=\"p_195\">102.          Five hundred years from now, some deceptive\n\nelements gave undue importance to cow safety. On the other\n\nhand the decline of Muslim power gave vent to this\n\ntreacherous and knave propaganda.            In this way these\n\ncunning elements ruined the Muslim economy, created\n\nanimosity between the two communities and dishonoured\n\nIslam.   A` huge barrier has been raised against the\n\npropogation of Islam. Staunch meat eating communities like\n\nMarathas and Rajputs who do not even spare pork, have\n\nsevered ties from muslims because of this problem and\n\nconsidered the muslims untouchables. These mischieveous\n\npeople by their clandestine propaganda have not just\n\nproduced aversion from cow sacrifice but also fermented\n\nviolence on the occasion of cow sacrifice.     They are aware\n\nthat these illogical prolonged campaign has shut the eyes of\n\nthe people.     Then he stated that the Hindus shall be\n\ncautioned:\n</p><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_128\">                          123</span><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_196\">\n\n\n\n\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_115\">&quot;O bevildered &amp; deceived bretheren, try to\nconceive this fact, by despising those who\nsacrifice the cow you actually despise Sri Ramji &amp;\nSita Devi because they sacrificed 1000 cows at\nriver Ganges - Refer Walmiki Puran.             Refer\nBrahmadi Vrat Puran, you in fact hate Manuji,\nwho is the avtar of Dharam Shastra, because he\nhad sacrificed 5 lakh cows at river Narbada.\nRefer the Bhagvat Puran, you are not despising\nmuslims but in fact you despise Sri Krishanji and\nArjunji because they sacrificed a cow at the\noccasion of Yag (Animal Sacrifice). Refer Utram\nCharitra you would comprehend you hate not the\nmuslims but you actually hate Walmik Rishi &amp;\nVashishta Muni because they used to sacrifice\ncows &amp; consumed its flesh in Walmik Ashram.\nIn short there is not a single rishi mentioned in\nVedas     shastras   &amp;   puranas   who    has    not\nconsumed beef. Those people are accountable in\nfront of Allah who raged hindu sentiments by this\nfallacious propaganda of cow safety and caused\nviolence and blood shed of thousands of people,\nand looked down upon the conduct of the World\nteacher    Prophet   Hazrat    Mohammad    (Pbuh),\ndishonoured Islam and hurt muslim sentiments.&quot;\n</blockquote><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_129\">                               124</span>\n\n\n\n\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_197\">According to him:\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_116\">       &quot;Hinduism is not a religion in the real sense as\n       this name is neither found in any of the vedas nor\n       it is written in shastras, puranas etc.   Inhabitant\n       of Indian peninsula is a Hindu-They extract\n       undue advantage from this name. Of the various\n       religions in India no two of them would be devoid\n       of differences in their basic tenets. To forge unity\n       they acquired the name &#x27;HINDU&#x27; and gave\n       leverage to a baseless issue of cow safety.      To\n       annul this, muslims should exert maximum efforts\n       which would end the stratagem of diabolic\n       elements. Only this remedy is a strong means to\n       substantiate the identity and muslim entity in\n       India. Another real means for them to sustain in\n       India is to repeatedly present the clear image and\n       nice conduct of Prophet Hazrat Mohammad (Pbuh)\n       to the hindus.&quot;\n</blockquote>\n\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_198\">This book is written in the year 1929.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_199\">\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_200\">103.         Then we have another book titled &quot;Lingayath&quot;\n\nwritten    by     Hazrath     Maulana      Siddiqui     Deendar\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_130\">                                           125</span>\n\n\n\n\nChannabasaveshwararu.                    It is in Kannada. The relevant\n\nportion extracted by the Trial Court is as under:-\n\n\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_117\">                                       &quot;\u00b0AU\u00c1Ai\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\n      \u00a5\u00c9\u00c3e\u00ef-4\n               ...................    \u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00e4      \u00b8\u00c0\u00ab\u00c4\u00c3\u00a5\u00c0z\u00c0      \u00b8\u00c0A\u00a7A\u00a2v\u00c0     \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\u00c4\u00db\n      \u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00e4\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df G\u00bd\u00b9 \u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00e4 \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00c6\u00ae\u00aa\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df \u00a8s\u00c0z\u00c0\u00e6 \u00a5\u00c0r\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0 z\u00c1\u00e6\u00ab\u00c3q\u00c0\n      d\u00a3\u00c1AU\u00c0z\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0 \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00ba\u00c1 \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4Av\u00c0\u00e6\u00aa\u00c1z\u00c0 &quot; \u00ba\u00c0g\u00c0\u00ba\u00c0g\u00c0 \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00ba\u00c1z\u00c9\u00c3\u00aa\u00c0&quot; Ez\u00c0\u00ddz\u00c0\u00c4\u00dd\n      \u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00e4 Jz\u00c0\u00c4g\u00c1\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4. \u00a5\u00c0\u00e6\u00a7\u00aeg\u00c1z\u00c0 \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00c6\u00aeP\u00c0 v\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00e4 \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00ba\u00c1\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4Av\u00c0\u00e6\u00aa\u00c1z\u00c0\n      &quot;\u00ba\u00c0j\u00ba\u00c0j \u00b9\u00c3v\u00c1g\u00c1\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4&quot; JAz\u00c0\u00c4 \u00a5\u00c0p\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0Av\u00c9 \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1q\u00c0 \u00ba\u00c0w\u00dbz\u00c1\u00ddg\u00c9.\n      CAz\u00c0g\u00c9 \u00ba\u00c0g\u00c0d\u00a3\u00c0g\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df \u00ba\u00c0jd\u00a3\u00c0g\u00c0\u00a3\u00c1\u00dfV \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1q\u00c0\u00ba\u00c0w\u00dbz\u00c1\u00ddg\u00c9. \u00b8\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1d\u00aa\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df \u00a8\u00c9\u00bc\u00c9\u00b9P\u00c9\u00c6\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4\u00ee\u00aa\u00c0 Gz\u00c9\u00dd\u00c3\u00b1\u00c0\u00a2Az\u00c0\n      DAi\u00c0\u00c4\u00e5\u00f0d\u00a3\u00c1AU\u00c0z\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4,          E\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df C\u00aa\u00c0jAz\u00c0 \u00aa\u00c9\u00c3z\u00c0U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0 \u00a5\u00c0\u00b1\u00c0\u00c4\u00ab\u00a3\u00c0 \u00a7\u00b0z\u00c1\u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5\n\u00a4Av\u00c0\u00c4\u00ba\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3\u00ac\u00c4v\u00c0\u00c4: \u00a8\u00cbz\u00c0\u00ddg\u00c0\u00c4 v\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00e4 D\u00bd\u00e9P\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4 P\u00c1\u00aez\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8 \u00aa\u00c9\u00ca\u00a2P\u00c0g\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df\n\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\u00db\u00b5\u00c0\u00c4\u00d6 \u00aa\u00c9\u00c4v\u00c0\u00dbU\u00c9 \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1rz\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4.            \u00aa\u00c1\u00b8\u00c0\u00db\u00aa\u00c0\u00aa\u00c1V F \u00b0AU\u00c1Ai\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\n\u00b8\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1dz\u00c0 \u00a5\u00c0\u00e6\u00a8s\u00c1\u00aa\u00c0\u00a2Az\u00c0 \u00bbAz\u00c0\u00c6 d\u00a3\u00c1AU\u00c0U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4 Jv\u00c0\u00db\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df \u00a5\u00c0\u00c6d\u00e5\n\u00ba\u00c1U\u00c0\u00c6 U\u00cbg\u00c0\u00aa\u00c1\u00ba\u00c0\u00f0\u00aa\u00c9Az\u00c0\u00c4 \u00a8s\u00c1\u00ab\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\u00db\u00b0\u00aa\u00c9. (\u00b9\u00a2\u00dd\u00c3P\u00c0) \u00a2\u00c3\u00a3\u00efz\u00c1g\u00c0g\u00c0\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_136\">                                  131</span>\n\n\n\n\n\u00b8\u00c0\u00e9g\u00c0\u00c6\u00a5\u00c0z\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8      \u00a5\u00c0\u00e6\u00b8\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00dfg\u00c1U\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3    DU\u00c0     F      \u00a7\u00b8\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0t\u00da\u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4\n\u00aa\u00c9\u00c4\u00caz\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3g\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4,    \u00ba\u00c1U\u00c0\u00c6      Cg\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0v\u00c0\u00c6\u00e4g\u00c0\u00c4    \u00b2\u00aa\u00c0   \u00b1\u00c0g\u00c0tg\u00c0\u00c4     P\u00c0\u00c6q\u00c0\ne\u00c9\u00c6v\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4\u00b0\u00e8 \u00a7g\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4.\n</blockquote>\n\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_131\">\u00a5\u00c9\u00c3e\u00ef - 17\n          Cx\u00c0\u00f0 -         \u00b8\u00c0\u00e9U\u00c0\u00f0, \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\u00e5\u00f0, \u00a5\u00c1v\u00c1\u00bc\u00c0U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4 \u00b0AU\u00c0z\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8\u00aa\u00c9.\n\u00ba\u00c1U\u00c0\u00c6 Z\u00c0g\u00c1Z\u00c0g\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4\u00dbU\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4 Ez\u00c0g\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8\u00aa\u00c9, \u00b0AU\u00c0z\u00c0 \u00ba\u00c9\u00c6g\u00c0U\u00c9 K\u00a3\u00c0\u00c6 E\u00ae\u00e8\nGz\u00c9\u00dd\u00c3\u00b1\u00c0\u00aa\u00c9\u00c3\u00a3\u00c9Az\u00c0g\u00c9, E\u00ba\u00c0\u00a5\u00c0g\u00c0U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c9g\u00c0q\u00c0g\u00c0 \u00aa\u00c1\u00e5\u00a5\u00c0P\u00c0\u00aa\u00c1Vz\u00c9.          \u00b0AU\u00c1Ai\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\nd\u00a3\u00c1AU\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5 Ez\u00c0g\u00c0 \u00ab\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0u\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4\u00b0\u00e8 \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1\u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00a4U\u00c9 \u00b8\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\u00dbz\u00c9. Cwy \u00a5\u00c0\u00c6e\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4 \u00aa\u00c9\u00c3\u00bc\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4\u00b0\u00e8 \u00aa\u00c9\u00c4\u00c3zs\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1q\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5z\u00c0P\u00c0\u00c6\u00cc \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\u00c4\u00db\n\u00b1\u00c1\u00e6z\u00c0\u00dd \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1q\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5z\u00c0P\u00c0\u00c6\u00cc U\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3\u00ba\u00c0v\u00c9\u00e5 \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1q\u00c0\u00a8\u00c9\u00c3P\u00c0\u00c4.\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_209\">5. \u00b1\u00c1\u00e6z\u00c0\u00ddz\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8 U\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3\u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1A\u00b8\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df \u00a8s\u00c0Q\u00eb\u00b9z\u00c0g\u00c9 MAz\u00c0\u00c4 \u00aa\u00c0\u00b5\u00c0\u00f0z\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c9U\u00c0\u00c6\n\u00a6v\u00c0\u00c8U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4 v\u00c0\u00c8\u00a6\u00dbAi\u00c0\u00c4\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df \u00ba\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3Az\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4.\n\n\n\u00a5\u00c9\u00c3e\u00ef - 134\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_210\">3. R\u00c4\u00b5\u00c0\u00e5\u00b1\u00c0\u00c8AU\u00c0 z\u00c9\u00c3\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c1d\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4 MAz\u00c0\u00c4 \u00a2\u00aa\u00c0\u00b8\u00c0 C\u00a3\u00c9\u00c3P\u00c0 \u00a5\u00c0\u00b1\u00c0\u00c4U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\nAi\u00c0\u00c4Y\u00d5 \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1r\u00b9z\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4.         C\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0 g\u00c0P\u00c0\u00db\u00a2Az\u00c0 Z\u00c0g\u00c0Ar \u00ba\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00eez\u00c0 \u00a4\u00c3g\u00c0\u00c4\nP\u00c9A\u00a5\u00c1V \u00ba\u00c0jAi\u00c0\u00c4\u00c4w\u00db\u00a2\u00ddv\u00c0\u00c4.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_211\">4. \u00a7\u00e6\u00ba\u00c1\u00e4\u00a2\u00aa\u00c0v\u00c0\u00f0 \u00a5\u00c0\u00c4g\u00c1tz\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8 \u00a7g\u00c9\u00a2g\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5z\u00c9\u00c3\u00a3\u00c9Az\u00c0g\u00c9 - \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\n\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00c4\u00a4\u00b8\u00c1\u00e9\u00ab\u00c4Ai\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4 \u00a3\u00c0g\u00c0\u00e2z\u00c1 \u00ba\u00c9\u00c6\u00bc\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4 z\u00c0ArAi\u00c0\u00c4\u00b0\u00e8 \u00b8\u00c1\u00abg\u00c1g\u00c0\u00c4\n\u00a5\u00c0\u00b1\u00c0\u00c4U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df Ai\u00c0\u00c4Y\u00d5 \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1r\u00b9z\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4.           Cz\u00c0g\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8 5 \u00aeP\u00c0\u00eb \u00ba\u00c0\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_145\">                                        140</span>\n\n\n\n\n\u00aa\u00c0zs\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df    \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1r\u00b9       \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00c6g\u00c0\u00c4        P\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3n       d\u00a3\u00c0jU\u00c9    \u00a8s\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3d\u00a3\u00c0\n\u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1r\u00b9z\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_212\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_213\">5. \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00ba\u00c1\u00a8s\u00c1g\u00c0v\u00c0 \u00aa\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00a5\u00c0\u00f0z\u00c0 207 \u00a3\u00c9\u00c3 Czs\u00c1\u00e5Ai\u00c0\u00c4z\u00c0 8 - 9 \u00a3\u00c9\u00c3\n\n\u00b1\u00c9\u00c6\u00e8\u00c3P\u00c0U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8     Eg\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0z\u00c9\u00c3\u00a3\u00c9Az\u00c0g\u00c9           -     g\u00c1d\u00a3\u00c1z\u00c0        \u00aa\u00c0z\u00c0AwAi\u00c0\u00c4\n\u00a5\u00c1P\u00c0\u00b1\u00c1\u00af\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4\u00b0\u00e8 \u00a2\u00a3\u00c0A\u00a5\u00c0\u00e6w Jg\u00c0q\u00c0\u00c4 \u00b8\u00c1\u00abg\u00c0 \u00a5\u00c0\u00b1\u00c0\u00c4U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4 \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c0\u00c4\u00db\nJg\u00c0q\u00c0\u00c4 \u00b8\u00c1\u00abg\u00c0 \u00ba\u00c0\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4 \u00aa\u00c0zs\u00c9 \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1q\u00c0\u00ae\u00e0q\u00c0\u00c4w\u00dbz\u00c0\u00dd\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5.\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_214\">6. \u00b9\u00c3v\u00c1z\u00c9\u00c3\u00abAi\u00c0\u00c4\u00c4 \u00b2\u00e6\u00c3g\u00c1\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00a3\u00c9\u00c6A\u00a2U\u00c9 \u00aa\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c1\u00b8\u00c0P\u00c9\u00cc z\u00c0Q\u00ebtP\u00c9\u00cc \u00ba\u00c9\u00c6g\u00c0l\nP\u00c1\u00aez\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8 U\u00c0AU\u00c1 \u00ba\u00c9\u00c6\u00bc\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4 \u00aa\u00c9\u00c4\u00c3\u00af\u00c9 \u00a7Az\u00c0\u00c4 &quot; J\u00af\u00c9\u00ca U\u00c0AU\u00c9Ai\u00c9\u00c4, \u00a3\u00c1\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5\nF \u00aa\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00aa\u00c1\u00b8\u00c0\u00a2Az\u00c0 \u00a5\u00c0\u00c4\u00a3\u00c0: \u00a7Az\u00c0g\u00c9 MAz\u00c0\u00c4 \u00b8\u00c1\u00abg\u00c0 U\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df\n\u00aa\u00c0zs\u00c9 \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1r\u00b9 Ov\u00c0t \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1r\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c9\u00db\u00c3\u00a3\u00c9.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_215\">\n\u00a5\u00c9\u00c3e\u00ef - 135\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_216\">1.        \u00af\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3P\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1\u00a3\u00c0\u00e5 w\u00aeP\u00c0g\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4 \u00a7g\u00c9\u00c6\u00c3q\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4 P\u00c1\u00a3\u00ef\u00a5s\u00c0g\u00c9\u00a4\u00ec\u00a3\u00c0\u00b0\u00e8,\n          &quot;Jg\u00c0q\u00c0\u00c4 \u00b8\u00c1\u00abg\u00c0 \u00aa\u00c0\u00b5\u00c0\u00f0U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0 \u00bbAz\u00c9 \u00bbAz\u00c0\u00c6 d\u00a3\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4 v\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00e4\n          zs\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1\u00f0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00b8\u00c1g\u00c0\u00aa\u00c1V                 \u00a5\u00c0\u00b1\u00c0\u00c4\u00aa\u00c0zs\u00c9Ai\u00c0\u00c4\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df       \u00aa\u00c0i\u00c1r\n          \u00a8s\u00c0Q\u00eb\u00b8\u00c0\u00c4w\u00dbz\u00c0\u00ddg\u00c0\u00c4.            C\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0       g\u00c0P\u00c0\u00db\u00a2Az\u00c0     \u00a3\u00c0\u00a2U\u00c0\u00bc\u00c0\u00c4\n          P\u00c9A\u00a5\u00c1U\u00c0\u00c4w\u00dbz\u00c0\u00dd\u00aa\u00c0\u00c5&quot; JAz\u00c0\u00c4 \u00ba\u00c9\u00c3\u00bdz\u00c0g\u00c0\u00c4.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_217\">\u00a5\u00c9\u00c3e\u00ef-214\n          ............\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00c8v\u00c0\u00c4\u00e5AdAi\u00c0\u00c4     \u00b8\u00c0\u00c6P\u00c0\u00eb\u00e4v\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00e4d\u00f0U\u00c0z\u00c1\u00e9\u00e5\u00a6     dU\u00c0z\u00c0\u00c4\u00ceg\u00c0\u00c4&quot;\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_151\">JA\u00a7 \u00a9g\u00c0\u00c4z\u00c0\u00a3\u00c0\u00c4\u00df \u00a5\u00c0q\u00c9z\u00c0 \u00ba\u00c0dg\u00c0v\u00ef \u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00ba\u00c0\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00e4z\u00ef \u00a5\u00c9\u00caU\u00c0A\u00a7g\u00c0g\u00c9 dU\u00c0w\u00db\u00a3\u00c0\nzs\u00aa\u00c0\u00c4\u00f0U\u00c0\u00bdU\u00c0\u00c6 C\u00a2\u00fcP\u00c1jAi\u00c0i\u00c1Vg\u00c0\u00c4v\u00c1\u00dbg\u00c9.&quot;\n</blockquote><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_146\">                                   141</span>\n\n\n\n\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_218\">105.         There is yet another book by name Panch\n\nShanthi Marg marked as Ex.P.131 where the teachings of\n\nthis   Maulana       Siddique      Channabassaveshwararu        as\n\nunderstood by his disciples has been set-out.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_219\">106.         In the book &#x27;Jagadguru&#x27; how the Hindu Gurus,\n\nwho called themselves as Jagadgurus do not possess\n\nrequisite qualification is set out. It says that none of them\n\nare Jagadgurus. According to this book,             only Hazarath\n\nMohammed Paigamber is the Jagadguru. Then there is also\n\nreference to Hindu religion, as a religion that recognizes\n\neating of flesh.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Issue\" id=\"p_220\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_221\">107.         From the aforesaid literature it is clear that the\n\nwhole object of the organization is to propagate that Islam is\n\nthe perfect religion and Quran is the complete book; Quran\n\nhas certified Muslims as the leaders of humanity.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_222\">108.
