A/c bearing\n\nNo.16602; Ex.P70 is the A/c opening form submitted by A-\n\n10 and Ex.P71 is his specimen signature card. Ex.P.72 is the\n\ncertified computer printout of ledger pertaining to A-10&#x27;s\n\nS.B. A/c. It is in the evidence of this witness that A-10&#x27;s wife\n\nalso had an S.B.A/c bearing No.17153; he has identified\n\nEx.P73, P74 and P75 to be of A-10&#x27;s wife&#x27;s A/c opening form,\n\nher specimen signature card and the computer printout of\n\nsaid S.B. Account ledger.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_648\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_649\">339.           As seen from the evidence on record, on\n\n15.10.2000, the IO recorded the statement of Manjunath,\n\nthe Assistant Manager of Standard Chartered Bank and also\n\nreceived the extract of the statement of accounts of A.10, as\n\nper Ex.P112 and his request is at Ex.P113. On 19.09.2000,\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_417\">                              364</span>\n\n\n\n\nhe went to Bangalore Merchantile Bank, Shivajinagar\n\nBranch, enquired the Bank Manager, Mallikarjun and\n\nrecorded his statement. Ex.P114 is the request made to the\n\nManager and Ex.P115 is the extracts of the accounts details\n\npertaining to A.10, which was given by the Bank.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_650\">340.        On 22.09.2000, IO gave a requisition to Varthur\n\nGrama Panchayath as per Ex.P116 seeking information as to\n\nthe owner of the house in which A.10 was residing and\n\nEx.P117 is the particulars furnished by the Secretary of the\n\nGrama Panchayath. On 27.01.2001, the IO has obtained\n\nparticulars of statement of accounts of A-10 with the Citi\n\nBank as per Ex.P143 and thereby got to know that A-10 not\n\nonly had the Visa Card facility but also an additional card in\n\nhis wife Sayeeda Barkath Unnisa&#x27;s name. In addition to this\n\nA-10&#x27;s wife also possessed a Master card as well as Suvidha\n\ncard and A.10 had also given another additional card each to\n\nA-16, who is absconding and A-12&#x27;s son Syed Siddique\n\nHussian.\n</p><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_418\">                             365</span><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_651\">\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_652\">341.        Thereafter the IO obtained the particulars of\n\ncredit card facility availed by A-10 from Standard Chartered\n\nBank which is as per Ex.P112 and Ex.P113 and found that\n\nA-10 has got an additional credit card from the said bank\n\nStandard Chartered Bank in the name of A.4 by describing\n\nhim as a brother. But, A-4 is the son of absconding A-1, who\n\nis absconding.   Ex.P112 and P113 disclose that around\n\n17.10.1999, A-4 using the said credit card had withdrawn a\n\nsum of Rs.500/- from HSBC Bank, Banjara Hills, Hyderabad\n\nand Rs.2,000/- on 22.10.1999; Rs.5,000/- and Rs.9000/-\n\non 22.02.2000 from ANZ Bank, Peshawar Branch, Pakistan;\n\nRs.8,000/ on 15.05.2000 from Citibank ATM, Ravalpindi\n\nBranch, Pakistan using the same card; and all these\n\namounts are debited to the account of A-10.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_653\">342.        On 12.08.1999, A-10&#x27;s cheque for Rs.300/-\n\ndrawn on Mercantile Bank, Bangalore was credited to the\n\naccount of A-12 in Canara Bank, Nandi Durga Branch as\n\nseen from Ex.P64. Even in Ex.P115, there is a reference to\n\nthese transactions.   On 04.02.2000, A-10 issued a cheque\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_419\">                              366</span>\n\n\n\n\nfor Rs.524/- and Rs.600 on 12.05.2000 drawn on Amanath\n\nCo-operative Bank, Gangenahalli Branch, Bangalore in\n\nfavour of A.12, as seen from Ex.P48 and the said amount\n\nwas credited to the account of A-12 as per the entries in\n\nEx.P49. It is relevant to mention here that deceased A-15\n\nhad introduced A-12 while opening an account in Canara\n\nBank, Nandi Durga Road Branch.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_654\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_655\">343.        I.O., V.S.D&#x27; Souza PW57&#x27;s cross-examination is\n\ndirected against his knowledge about the procedures to be\n\nfollowed before obtaining a credit card and it is also\n\nsuggested that all the transactions of A-10 are legal as he\n\nhad filed returns to the Income-tax Department. Thus the\n\nevidence of PW57 regarding the Bank accounts and credit\n\ncards held by A.10 and additional cards, which he got to his\n\nwife and other accused, the issue of cheques in favour of the\n\nother accused and encashment of the same and crediting of\n\nthe amount to the accounts of the other accused from his\n\naccount are all virtually not disputed.\n</p><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_420\">                                367</span><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_656\">\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_657\">344.         In the course of <a href=\"/doc/767287/\" id=\"a_363\">Section 313</a> Cr.P.C statement A-\n\n10 has stated that his yearly income to be Rs.2,50,000/-.\n\nBut it is interesting to note that this accused had opened\n\naccount in more than eleven banks at various places in\n\nBangalore City and enjoyed Debit card and Credit card\n\nfacility. He had even provided additional credit cards to his\n\nwife and also to A-4 and A-16, who are facing trial in this\n\ncase.    A-4 has withdrawn certain amount using such\n\nadditional credit card from various places in Pakistan. This\n\nonly goes to show that A-10 had close contact with other co-\n\naccused.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_658\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_659\">345.         Similarly other important documentary evidence\n\nwhich shows the complicity of A10 in the commission of the\n\noffence is his telephone and mobile connections.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_660\">346.         On 09.10.2000, PW57 obtained the print-out of\n\ncall    details   pertaining    to   A-10&#x27;s   Airtel   Mobile\n\nNo.9845044537, from Airtel Officials as per Ex.P157. Apart\n\nfrom this mobile phone connection, A-10 had two land line\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_421\">                               368</span>\n\n\n\n\nconnections bearing No.8538159 and 5711064 in his name\n\nand the third connection bearing No.8538537 in his father-\n\nin-law&#x27;s name; A-10&#x27;s residence had the connections bearing\n\nNo. 8538159 and 8538537 whereas the third landline\n\nbearing No. 5711064 however was in another house bearing\n\nNo.1,   East    Street,   Annayyappa   Garden,   Neelasandra,\n\nBangalore. During enquiry, it was found that A-10 is the\n\nbrother of absconding A-16.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_661\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_662\">347.           P.W.40 - L.R.R.Swamy, Vigilance Officer, BSNL,\n\nBangalore during the period 2001-2003 has deposed that\n\nthe III Additional CMM, Bangalore asked him to furnish the\n\nparticulars of some telephone numbers by giving a memo,\n\nwhich was handed over to him by the police as per Ex.P54\n\nand accordingly he furnished the particulars at Ex.P55.\n\nEx.P57 shows that the telephone No.8538537 is in the name\n\nof Sattar Khan PW14, whose house is situated near Sharada\n\nVidya Mandir at Varthur which means that being residents\n\nof that house A-10 and his wife PW16 were using this\n\ntelephone connection; numbers mentioned in the call log\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_422\">                             369</span>\n\n\n\n\nEx.P57 were called by A-10 and his wife. As could be seen\n\nfrom Ex.P57, from land telephone phone No.8538537 calls\n\nwere made on most of the times to A-10&#x27;s mobile phone\n\nbearing No.9845044537. Ex.P124 is the call log details of A-\n\n10&#x27;s mobile phone No. 9845044537 collected on 09.10.2000\n\nand Ex.P157 is the photo copy thereof. This is the\n\ncircumstantial evidence against A.10.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_663\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_664\">348.        A-7 in his confessional statement at Ex.P245\n\nhas extensively referred to the role of A-10 in hatching the\n\nconspiracy and executing the object sought to be achieved.\n\nHe has spoken about the presence of A.10 in the 3rd day of\n\nUrs in September 1999 at 7.40 hours at the premises of\n\nDeendar Anjuman Ashram, Asif Nagar, Hyderabad. At para\n\n21 of the confessional statement, he has clearly set out the\n\nother persons who were present in the said meeting. Again\n\nin April, 2000, A.6 and A.7, went to Bangalore for meeting\n\nChote Mulla at R.T.Nagar. From there they went to A.10&#x27;s\n\nhouse and made the floppy showing the Air location, which\n\nA.7 had taken from SQN leader Vasudevan&#x27;s cupboard. From\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_423\">                                  370</span>\n\n\n\n\nthere, they went to A.11&#x27;s house. On the way, A.10 told that\n\nhe had purchased a van and it is under repair. After dinner,\n\nA.10 and A.13 left their house. A.7 handed over the floppy\n\nwhich was brought from A.10, to A.11. Again in May, 2000,\n\nlast week or second week, they went to A.11&#x27;s house directly.\n\nThereafter, they met A.10 with others. On 22nd May, 2000,\n\nat   night   around    22.00     hours,   A.24   had   come       from\n\nHyderabad and A.2 came from Pakistan to see them. Then\n\nthey took Tata Sumo and went to Hyderabad. While coming\n\non the way, they rang up A.10 and others. They came next\n\nday and met at Anwar @ Hanif&#x27;s house. There all of them\n\nwere made to sit and he told that if anybody does Sarria,\n\nthat amount should be completely handed over to A.6 and\n\nfrom that their expenditure, if required, should be taken\n\nback and that all of them had to work collectively and there\n\nshould not be any misunderstanding. A.10 was present\n\nthroughout    the     meeting.   On    23.05.2000,     as   per    the\n\ntelephone call made by A.7 to A.10 as per the direction of\n\nabsconding A.2, A.10 immediately went to Nuzvid and met\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_424\">                                  371</span>\n\n\n\n\nabsconding A.2 and finalised the Church program. Further,\n\nin the house of one Anwar, A.2 instructed A.10 and other\n\nmembers present as to, how to spend the mobilized amount\n\nin the organization activities. On 20.06.2000, deceased A.6,\n\nA.2 and others went to the house of A.10 at Bangalore and\n\nthere they finalised the Mosque plan. Then A.18 presented\n\nthe chart prepared by him as per the instructions of A.2\n\nwherein, the action plans of various programs were written\n\nand the work of each program was entrusted to the members\n\nof the Deendar Anjuman Organization.              At that time, A.10\n\nhimself cooked the food and served all the persons.                  They\n\nfinalised the plan to secure arms, ammunitions and\n\nexplosives either from Bengal side or Jaisalmer (Rajasthan)\n\nside.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_665\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_666\">349.        The      evidence    of   this   PW.54       Abdul Gafoor\n\ncorroborates   the     facts    narrated     by   this    A.7   in    his\n\nconfessional statement Ex.P.245 as stated above. Hence, on\n\nthe basis of the oral and documentary evidence in our\n\nopinion the prosecution has duly proved the overt acts\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_425\">                             372</span>\n\n\n\n\ncommitted by this A.10 and his involvement in this banned\n\nDeendar Anjuman Organization.        On the basis of the\n\nevidence this A.10 S.M. Ibrahim has regularly visited this\n\nbanned Deendar Anjuman Ashram Hyderabad, secretly met\n\nabsconding A.1 namely Zia Ul Hassan and his sons in that\n\nAshram, he actively participated and became party to the\n\nconspiracy meetings held at Hyderabad, as well as in his\n\nhouse at Bangalore and in the house of A.11 Abdul\n\nRehaman Sait at Bangalore and in the house of A.25 at\n\nBhatakurki, in the house of A-26 at Hubli and in his\n\npresence A.1 secretly appointed deceased accused No.6 and\n\nA.7 as Zonal Chiefs of the Deendar Anjuman Organization,\n\nrecorded the information about secret vital installations in\n\nthe floppies, e-mailed the same to A.1 r/o Pakistan, on\n\n23-5-2000 as per the telephone call made by his A.7 to this\n\nA.10 as per the direction of absconding A.2 this A.10\n\nimmediately went to Nuzvid and met absconding A.2 and\n\nfinalised the church programme and further in the house of\n\none Anwar A.2 instructed this A.10 and other members\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_426\">                             373</span>\n\n\n\n\npresent there how to spend the mobilized amount in the\n\norganization activities. On 20-06-2000 deceased A.6 A.2 and\n\nothers went to the house of this A.10 Ibrahim, there they\n\nfinalised the mosque plan, then A.18 presented a chart\n\nprepared by him as per the instruction of A.2 wherein the\n\naction plans of various programmes were written and the\n\nwork of each programme was entrusted to the members of\n\nthe Deendar Anjuman Organisation. At that time this A.10\n\nhimself cooked the food and served all the persons and\n\nfurther they finalised the plan to secure arms, ammunitions\n\nand explosives either from Bengal side or from Jaisalmer\n\n(Rajasthan) side. All these overt acts committed by this A.10\n\nshows that he was one of the active member of the banned\n\nDeendar Anjuman Organisation and to achieve its aims and\n\nobjectives i.e., to Islamise the whole of India he has\n\ncommitted all these jihad, nifaaq and siria activities apart\n\nfrom becoming party to the conspiracies. He was the driver\n\nof the Maruthi Van. He went near the Church along with\n\nA.14 and A.14 for planting the bomb in the Church, by\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_427\">                              374</span>\n\n\n\n\ncarrying the bombs in the Maruthi Van. He is a party to the\n\nconspiracy at various places in Karnataka.        He is already\n\nconvicted by the Hyderabad Court for the said offence of\n\ncriminal conspiracy, which judgment has become final.\n\nThus the prosecution has proved all the charges levelled\n\nagainst him beyond reasonable doubt.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_667\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_668\">350.         Now, we have to see what is the role played by\n\neach of the remaining accused in this incident.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_669\">351.         Absconding accused A-1 to A-6 are all residents\n\nof Mardan, Pakistan.     The Investigating Officer tried to\n\nsecure them through Interpol. But in spite of his efforts, he\n\ncould not secure them. On completion of the investigation,\n\nthe Invetigating Officer submitted the final report on\n\n19.03.2001    before   the   learned   3rd   Additional   Chief\n\nMetropolitan Magistrate, Bangalore against 29 accused\n\npersons showing A.1 to A.5, A.16 &amp; A.29 as absconding,\n\nA.14 and A.15 as dead for the offences punishable under\n\nSections 124-A, 153-A, 295, 436, 427 read with <a href=\"/doc/1128948/\" id=\"a_364\">Section 120-</a>\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_428\">                               375</span>\n\n\n\n\nB of IPC and also under <a href=\"/doc/1056565/\" id=\"a_365\">Sections 3</a>, <a href=\"/doc/895566/\" id=\"a_366\">4</a> and <a href=\"/doc/1335368/\" id=\"a_367\">5</a> of the Indian\n\nExplosive Substance Act, 1908 and under <a href=\"/doc/94717/\" id=\"a_368\">Section 5</a> read\n\nwith <a href=\"/doc/176528/\" id=\"a_369\">Section 9B</a> of the Explosives Act, 1884.     The learned\n\nMagistrate by an order dated 04.07.2005 commited this\n\nCase to the Sessions Court under <a href=\"/doc/887219/\" id=\"a_370\">Section 209</a> Cr.P.C. in\n\nrespect A.7 to A.13 and A.18 to A.27 after splitting that case\n\nagainst absconding A.1 to A.5, A.16 and A.29 and another\n\ncase in C.C. No.18980/2005 was registered against those\n\nabsconding accused.        Therefore, as the A.1 to A.5 are\n\nabsconding, no trial took place in these proceedings. Insofar\n\nas A.6 is concerned, he died during the pendency of the trial.\n\nHowever, the evidence on record discloses that absconding\n\nA.1 is the eldest son of founder of Deendar Anjuman Ashram\n\ni.e.,   Late    Hazarath    Jagadguru   Channabasaveshwara\n\nSiddique.      The absconding A.2 to A.5 are the sons of A.1.\n\nAfter the death of Hazarath Siddique, his elder son i.e., A.1\n\nbecame the Guru and head of Deendar Anjuman Ashram,\n\nHyderabad. Later, along with all the members of his family,\n\nhe went to Pakistan and stayed in Mardan and became the\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_429\">                              376</span>\n\n\n\n\nCitizen of that country. However, every year, he used to visit\n\nHyderabad along with his family members at the time of\n\ncelebration of Urs of his late father.   P.W.47 - Syed Afsar\n\nAhamed and P.W.53 - Syed Shahanawaz Ahamed, who are\n\nfull brothers residing at Asif Nagar, Hyderabad have spoken\n\nabout the details of the wives of Late Hazarath Siddique.\n\nThey have also spoken about Late Hazarath Siddique and\n\nalso about where he was residing during his life time and\n\nafter his death, how the Urs was being performed in that\n\nAshram. It is evident from the documents Ex.P.107 to 111\n\ncollected by P.W.57 - Investigating Officer, during the course\n\nof investigation, that in the year 1999, absconding A.1 held a\n\nconspiracy meeting along with his followers.      Their main\n\nconspiracy was to Islamize whole of India by committing\n\nJihad, Nefaq and Siria.      A.1 was regulating all these\n\nactivities from Pakistan itself. He trained so many Muslim\n\nyoungsters in Arms and Ammunitions including explosive\n\nsubstances, set their minds to commence and continue\n\ncommunal disturbances and destroy the vital installations of\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_430\">                               377</span>\n\n\n\n\nIndia and secretely attack the defence force in India, to derail\n\nthe Indian Ecomomy and secretely attack the defence force\n\nof India including Airforce, Army and Navy.           For this\n\npurpose, he secured Officers serving in Indian Air force,\n\nArmy, Railway and other field, made them the members of\n\nthe organization. Ex.P.107 to Ex.P.111 are the immigration\n\nregistration extracts pertaining to the visit of A.1 and his\n\nfamily members to India. A.1 to A.5 having come to India,\n\napart from meeting their relatives and performing Urs of Late\n\nHazarath Siddique, they conducted conspiracy meeting in\n\nHyderabad as well as in various places in Karnataka\n\nincluding Bangalore. The entire evidence of P.W.54 and the\n\nentire confession statement of A.7     as per Ex.P.245 shows\n\nthe firm determination made by A.1 to A.5 to Islamize the\n\nwhole of India and for achieving that goal, the various acts\n\nencouraged by them like Siria, i.e., theft, robbery, dacoity,\n\nNifaq i.e., creation of communal disturbance, Jihad i.e.,\n\ndeclaration of war against India, to Islamise whole of India.\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_431\">                             378</span>\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_670\">352.        A.6 is the elder brother of A.7. The evidence on\n\nrecord shows that in September, 1999, A.1 discussed with\n\nA.6 and A.5 and later in the presence of others told that in\n\n1998, he had been to Makka/Madina, where he got the voice\n\nof Allaha that within two years, India would become an Islam\n\nState, which Deendar Anjuman had dreamt of seeing a\n\nreligious State. He further told that there would be a war\n\nbetween India and Pakistan and Pakistan would burn for\n\neight days and northern India would loose 70 to 75% and\n\nthat all arms and ammunitions would extinguish.     At that\n\ntime, A.1 would come with his 9 lakhs followers (pathans) to\n\nIndia and that when he keeps his leg on Punjab, then all has\n\nto make Hungama, Dhamaka in Southern States, because\n\nSouthern States would be powerful. If these things are done,\n\nthen it would be easy for him to come to Southern India. He\n\nhad talked with all the persons and told them to inform him\n\nin the Urs and that they had to attend Urs.     Further, he\n\nstated that A.6 would be seeing all these things, but A.7\n\nhave to advise and give necessary instructions if needed and\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_432\">                              379</span>\n\n\n\n\nthe details would be discussed in Urs meeting. The evidence\n\non record shows that A.6 proposed for a second hand vehicle\n\non finance to be tried.   He took a loan of Rs.40,000/- on\n\ninterest, went to Batkurki and handed over the amount. In\n\nJanuary, 2000 A.6 and A.7 went to Hyderabad and attended\n\nthe meeting of third day Urs in A-19&#x27;s flat. There, A.6 told\n\nthe persons to co-operate with A.24, as he had been selected\n\nby A.1 and that they had to damage Railways, IOC, PCI,\n\nElectric(grid), Government Installations by Saria Road, TPC.\n\nFurther, the evidence shows that A.6 attended several\n\nmeetings where conspiracies were hatched.      A.6 went to\n\nHubli and met A.26 and P.W.54 and discussed about the\n\nprocurement of revolvers.   He proposed to go to Bangalore\n\nand Batakurki.     Khasim brought the &#x27;G&#x27; stick, cable,\n\ndetonators and handed them to A.6, which he kept in mango\n\ngarden and paid money. He is the commander-in-chief. He\n\nwas prosecuted in Hyderabad case and was convicted. He\n\nwas under detention.        During the pendency of these\n\nproceedings, he died.\n</p><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_433\">                                380</span><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_671\">\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_672\">353.        A.7 is the person, who has given a confessional\n\nstatement, i.e., an extra-judicial confession.        Already, we\n\nhave discussed and pointed out how this confessional\n\nstatement is admissible in evidence. But it does not mean\n\nmerely   because    the   said   confessional    statement     is\n\nadmissible, the facts stated therein are all proved. Once this\n\nconfessional statement is held to be proved and made\n\nvoluntarily, it becomes a very important piece of evidence to\n\nprove the charges and the first test is satisfied. Therefore,\n\nthe Court should carefully examine the said evidence and\n\ncompare it with the rest of the evidence, in the light of the\n\nsurrounding circumstances and probabilities of the case.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"PetArg\" id=\"p_673\">354.        A   reading   of   A-7&#x27;s   confessional    statement\n\ndiscloses that he has set out in detail his family background,\n\navocation of his father, the number of family members, who\n\nare all studying and how he had to work during his studies.\n\nHe is from the place of Nuzvid in Andhra Pradesh. He was\n\nselected by the Indian Air Force in the year 1968; he married\n\nin the year 1973 and he has one daughter and a son. His\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_434\">                              381</span>\n\n\n\n\nson is decently employed and his daughter is married. He\n\nhas been awarded a medal for CAS on 26th January, 1992.\n\nOn the day he was arrested, he was stationed at Air Head\n\nquarters, R.K.Pura, New Delhi - 66. He states that his grand\n\nfather, father, his brothers and himself are all the followers\n\nof Deendar Anjuman, located at Hyderabad. It was founded\n\nby Hazrath Sayed Siddique Saheb in 1924. During his\n\nchildhood, he used to see Deendar&#x27;s followers (preachers),\n\nwho used to come to his home. They always talked about\n\nIslam comparing it with Bhagavatgita, Vedas, Ramayana,\n\nBible, Kalkipurana and Kalagyanam. It is his blind belief,\n\nwhich made him think that whatever they tell is true and\n\nfinal and he has to work as per their advice.      A-1 is the\n\nbeloved son of Hajarath Sayed Siddique Saheb. He was given\n\nmore importance and everyone followed whatever he said.\n\nWhile speaking to A-1 they never raised their heads and only\n\nlooked to his feet. Whatever he has done is not for any\n\nmonetary benefits, but only in blind belief and half\n\nknowledge. Now he has realized that how dangerous it is and\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_435\">                                382</span>\n\n\n\n\ntherefore has been repenting day and night. He got into bad\n\ncompany in the year 1991, when he attended Urs function\n\nfor the first time with his family members. The first day they\n\ntook blessings from A.1. He introduced himself as working in\n\nIndian Air Force. In the evening he also took `Baith&#x27;. The\n\nthird   day   evening   when    he   and   his   brothers   were\n\nchitchatting with friends regarding Urs, Mohinuddin came to\n\nhis brother A.6 and told that Hazarath was calling him as\n\nwell as A-6 from rear gate to his house. A.27 was pressing\n\nA.1&#x27;s legs. A.2 was sitting by the side of A.1. Mohinuddin\n\nwas standing, in his front, one Moulvi and one staff of HAL\n\nemployee were also sitting after taking his blessings. After\n\nasking A.27 to go away and after his departure, he told that\n\nhe has about 6 to 9 lakhs followers in Pakistan (Mardan). To\n\nshow them that he has followers in India in all places, he\n\nwants some information regarding defence services locations,\n\nso that he could show the information to his followers in\n\nMardan. Then A.7 gave him the particulars of where he had\n\nbeen posted earlier. He made the note of the same. Then he\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_436\">                             383</span>\n\n\n\n\nshowed Mohinuddin and asked him to furnish information to\n\nMohinuddin, so that, he will handover the same to A.1.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_674\">355.        In April/May 1991, when A-7 went to his house\n\nin Nuzvid, he met Mohinuddin and he got the information\n\nregarding Stock Holding Depot and he gave whatever\n\ninformation which was in his possession to Mohinuddin,\n\nwho noted the same. Later he heard that Mohiuddin got it\n\ntyped and got the same photocopied. In 1992 January, when\n\nthey met in Nuzvid, Mohinuddin asked him about the\n\nactivity of the Air Force and he gave information only about\n\nMaintenance Command, Nagpur. In his next trip to Nuzvid,\n\nhe took the Xerox copy of quarterly progress return of 1991\n\nDecember, which was in his Delhi cupboard and handed\n\nover the same to Mohinuddin at Nuzvid. Whenever he\n\ninquired with Mohinuddin, the purpose of such information,\n\nhe used to simply say that he is obtaining the same on the\n\ninstructions of Hazarath.   In 1992 May/June, Mohinuddin\n\nasked him about the Repair Depots and its places. Next day,\n\nhe brought 11 base Repaid Depot quarterly return copy and\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_437\">                             384</span>\n\n\n\n\ntold about others 1 BRD Chandighar, 30 WBRD Kanpur, 11\n\nBRD Nasik, 9 BRD Pune, 8 BRD Sulur to Mohinuddin, who\n\nnoted down the same and left that evening. He has also spoken about one\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_438\">                             385</span>\n\n\n\n\nMohamed Hussain. There, A.9 took IFT fuse wire and burnt it.\n\nAfter the demonstration, they proposed that if there is a\n\ntimer, then it is better. A.9 told that he would try to make\n\nthe timer. Then they all came back to home.      On terrace,\n\nthey all discussed about A.20&#x27;s program, which he had\n\nbrought from A1 in March 2000. He and A.R.Saith went to\n\ninternet shop and checked up as to any incoming mail was\n\nthere and then a letter was typed to A.1 about A.6&#x27;s son&#x27;s\n\nhealth. Next day, he and Anwar went to Internet and opened\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_450\">                              397</span>\n\n\n\n\nID on Syed Quasim Ali@USA.Net        with password `Sirajim-\n\nMunira&#x27;. They received four or five letters stating `Duva&#x27; for\n\nA.6&#x27;s son and were happy to see that. The next message was\n\nto warn the persons to work in unity. All the messages sent\n\nby A.11, A.6 or A.18 used to be addressed to `Qasim&#x27;.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_685\">362.        In first week of May 2000, A.18 came with two\n\nsketches and handed over to A.6 when he was in the\n\nhospital with his son. The same week, second lot of Gelatine\n\nsticks and electric detonators were brought by A.22 and\n\nKasim of Hujurnagar.    They were kept in Anwar @ Hanif&#x27;s\n\nhouse. A.6 told them that they should try to get local made\n\nexplosive powder (two types i.e., red and white). Later A.9\n\nwent to Hujurnagar and they brought it to his house and\n\nnext day all the items were given to Anwar @ Hanif to keep in\n\nhis house along with one round of .303, which A.7 had\n\npurchased from Devanandam in 1998. A.6 proposed to go to\n\nBangalore. So they two started for Bangalore by private bus\n\nin last or second week of May 2000. They had gone to A.11&#x27;s\n\nhouse directly. Then they met A.17, A.28, A.10 and A.12.\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_451\">                              398</span>\n\n\n\n\nA.11&#x27;s brother met and told salams. Thereafter they left to\n\nBhatakurki by Hubli fast passenger and reached next day\n\nevening.   Then A.6 gave lecture on Prophet Period and\n\npresent period. Then they went to A.25&#x27;s garden and tested\n\nthe powder. Just after prayer, A.6 told all the gathering that\n\nif A.1&#x27;s words do not come true by July, then the brothers\n\nwould quit from Deendar Ashram.          Gafoor from Solapur,\n\nSangli Basha and some Bhatakurki members were asked for\n\ndonations by A6, who told them to use for area only not\n\nrequired to give any one, if left, then remaining amount to be\n\npaid to Tata Sumo installment. Then when they reached to\n\nnearby town, Tailor Basha requested them to stay. A.6 told\n\nhim that he has to take the installment of Tata Sumo from\n\nthe collection of donations. They came back to Nuzvid. In\n\nBhatakurki,    A.25    showed      the    inability   regarding\n\nprocurement of revolvers. After reaching Nuzvid, A.13 was\n\ncalled to come down to Nuzvid. After two or three days A.13\n\ncame to Nuzvid. Then A.6 asked him about the procurement\n\nof revolvers. A.13 told that he has to go to Calcutta to search\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_452\">                                  399</span>\n\n\n\n\nfor someone who can get the revolvers.         So, he was paid\n\nRs.3,000/- and was told to give a ring if revolvers were\n\navailable and then money would be sent. After three days,\n\nA.13 gave a ring and asked that still he could not trace the\n\nperson, but his family required Rs.1,000/- urgently and\n\nrequested for TMO. After two or three days, A.6 sent TMO to\n\nA.13&#x27;s family. On 22nd May, 2000 night around 22.00 hours,\n\nA.24 of Hyderabad and A.2 came from Pakistan to see them.\n\nDuring early hours they took Tata Sumo and went to\n\nHyderabad.       On 23.05.2000, they returned to Nuzvid with\n\nA.2 by 22.30 hours. That day, he told that the program what\n\nA.20 has brought is to be implemented and anyhow he was\n\nhappy that Church program was a success and he told that\n\nhe would handle the program by talking to everyone\n\npersonally. He asked to do that in Mandir Program and gave\n\ndetails to A.9 and Maqbool. While coming on the way, they\n\nrang   up   to    A.10,   A.11   and   Abdul   Gafoor   to   come\n\nimmediately.      They came next day and met in Anwar @\n\nHanif&#x27;s house.     He made every one to sit and told that, if\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_453\">                             400</span>\n\n\n\n\nanybody does Sarria, that amount completely has to be\n\nhanded over to A.6 and from that their expenditure, if\n\nrequired, would be taken back and that all have to work\n\ncollectively, there should not be any misunderstanding. That\n\nnight Vijayawada Mandir blast was there.      Next day, he\n\npacked his luggage and moved to New Delhi.      He was not\n\naware as to who blasted the bomb and where it was\n\nprepared. When he had gone with DSP on 24.08.2000 to\n\nHyderabad, there he came to know that these were made at\n\nA.19&#x27;s flat in Mehadi Patnam. Regarding blast, he used to\n\nknow through newspaper or electronic media only. Then A.2\n\ntold that arms and ammunitions would be coming after he\n\nwould return to Pakistan. These things would come either\n\nfrom Bengal side or Rajasthan Jaisalmair side. A.2 further\n\ntold that arms and ammunitions would arrive to India and\n\nthe person, who would bring these things would contact\n\nHyderabad (he did not mention the name) and that\n\nHyderabad fellow would inform to A.6 at Nuzvid. Then he\n\nhad to arrange for collection from that person. The mode of\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_454\">                               401</span>\n\n\n\n\nconveyance was to be arranged and then he wanted to finish\n\nit off. This is the confessional statement of A.7.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_686\">363.        On reading the confessional statement, it can be\n\nsaid that A7 unequivocally confesses to have been a privy to\n\nthe criminal conspiracy collecting information from his place\n\nof work and handing over the same to A.1, collecting\n\nammunitions for the purpose of blasting and also Sarria\n\nconducted for raising funds sending a portion of the same to\n\nA.1 at Pakistan.   He has actively participated in the criminal\n\nconspiracy to wage war against India and this piece of\n\nevidence would be one of the most effective proofs in law. It\n\nis highly reliable because, no rational person would make\n\nadmission against his own interest unless prompted by his\n\nconscious to tell the truth. However, the rule of prudence\n\nrequires the Court to look for some corroboration of material\n\nparticulars. If there is part corroboration in conformity with\n\nthe general trend of confession, then that would be enough\n\nto hold the person, who has made confession, guilty of the\n\noffence to which he has confessed.\n</p><span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_455\">                                402</span><p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_687\">\n\n\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_688\">364.         Now let us see to what extent, the facts stated in\n\nthe confession is corroborated by other evidence on record.\n\nIn the <a href=\"/doc/767287/\" id=\"a_371\">Section 313</a> Cr.P.C. statement recorded in this case,\n\nwhen A7 was asked as to whether he had got anything to say\n\nabout this case, he has answered as under:\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_297\">       Q.274. Have you got anything more to say?\n       Ans:- I have been falsely implicated in this case.\n       I am very innocent, and I am sincere to my\n       mother land.\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_298\">             I have served for 33 years in Indian Air\n       Force, and when I was arrested I was Junior\n       Warrant Officer. I had been to Deendhar Anjuman\n       Organization twice for Urs like any other holy\n       places. I was aware about the five (5) principles\n       of   Deendhar     organization     including   Eko\n       Jagadguru, and Eko Jagadishwar, it means only\n       one Eshwar for one whole of the world. I don&#x27;t\n       have any literature about their principles.\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_689\">365.         The said statement is no explanation to the\n\nevidence that he       is a follower of     Deendar Anjuman\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_456\">                             403</span>\n\n\n\n\nOrganization, which is situated at Hyderabad and he was\n\nattending the Urs.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_690\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_691\">366.        P.W.54 - Abdul Gafoor has deposed that after\n\ntwo or three days he returned to Solapur after attending the\n\nfirst day of Urs in 1999, Basheer Sab Kokatnur, informed\n\nthat there was a meeting and in the meeting A.1 mentioned\n\nthat it was time for Jihad and would give instructions as to\n\nwhat everyone should and should not do, through A.7,\n\nBasheer Sab and A.25.      Pathans would be coming from\n\nNorth India and therefore they will be instructed as to what\n\nthey are expected to do.    During April, he along with ,\n\nBasheer Sab, A.25, Sangli Basha and Nabi Sab went to\n\nNuzvid by train, reached the house of A.6.     There, when\n\nBasheer requested for repayment of the money, A.6 informed\n\nthat A.7 would attend to that if they stayed back. The next\n\nday A.7 came and informed that he is not able to repay the\n\nmoney for another two months and paid Rs.4,500 towards\n\ninterest. A.7 also informed that Rs.30,000/- will be paid by\n\nNabi Sab and that they should not tell anybody about what\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_457\">                              404</span>\n\n\n\n\nthey were told. If they are not interested, they should keep\n\nquiet. But if they informed others, their life would be in\n\ndanger.    Tailor Basha and some Bank Officials expressed\n\ntheir inability and they left. A.7 gave money to all of them\n\ntowards travelling expenses. Once again, Basheer demanded\n\nfor money and A.7 told him that he would repay it within two\n\nmonths. From there P.W.54 claims to have gone to\n\nBhatakurki and Sangli where Nabi Sab paid Rs.30,000/-.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_692\">367.         P.W.57   -   V.S.D&#x27;Souza   is   the   Investigating\n\nOfficer.   He has deposed that on 01.08.2000, he went to\n\nHyderabad, secured the information from the Enquiry Officer\n\nin Crime No.35/2000 in relation to which, A.6, A.7 and\n\nothers had been arrested. He obtained the photographs of\n\nthose persons and thereafter visited Deendar Anjuman\n\nAshram, Hyderabad and secured the necessary particulars.\n\nOn 5.12.2000 in Bangalore Central Prison, he recorded the\n\nstatement of 7th accused.    On 24.09.2000 he secured the\n\npresence of witness Firoz Pasha and showed him the Album\n\nEx.P.31, said witness identified A.7 in the said photo and\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_458\">                                 405</span>\n\n\n\n\nstated to have seen A.7 visiting A.13. The photo so identified\n\nby the witness is marked as Ex.P.31(a).\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_693\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_694\">368.          Then we have the evidence of P.W.64 - M.B.\n\nAppanna, who continued the investigation and submitted\n\nthe charge sheet. He has produced the certified copy of the\n\nSeizure Mahazar-Ex.P.262 in this case, as the original was\n\nproduced in S.C.No.423/2001 and marked as Ex.P.273 in\n\nthat case. As per that seizure Mahazar, many incriminating\n\narticles were seized from the house of A.7 and were produced\n\nin     S.C.No.423/2001.        The    Investigating   Officer   had\n\nconducted mahazar at           60/3, Safed Sagar, Air Force\n\nQuarters belonging to A.7 situated at R.K. Puram, Delhi and\n\nin the presence of panchas, they have seized several\n\ndocuments. The relevant documents pertaining to the case\n\nare:\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_299\">        (1) Photograph (framed) of Guru Siddiq\n\n        (2) Asifnagar Urs Invitation with photographs of\n\n            Siddique and A.1\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_459\">                                406</span>\n\n\n\n\n       (3) One black diary of 1999 containing telephone\n\n           numbers\n\n       (4) Photo of A.1 of Pakistan\n\n       (5) One Oxford diary of 1998 (Blue colour) in which\n\n           literature and other writings pertaining to Siddiq\n\n           and photo of A.1 and 43 film negatives, were\n\n           seized.\n</blockquote>\n\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_695\">369.         The     Investigating   Officer,   P.W-64   -   M.B.\n\nAppanna, who was present at the time of the seizure had\n\ndeposed to that effect before the Court. They also produced\n\nthe certified copy of spot pointing out Mahazar Ex.P.259. Its\n\noriginal was produced in S.C.No.423/2001 and marked as\n\nEx.P166.   As per this Mahazar, A.7 pointed out the flat of\n\nA.19 at Hyderabad as the place where conspiracy meeting\n\nwas held in the presence of himself, his deceased younger\n\nbrother and other co-accused persons in that house. A.19 is\n\nsaid to have prepared the bombs since he was trained in\n\nMardan, Pakistan in arms and ammunitions and even in\n\npreparation of bombs.       P.W.22 - L.Lakshminarayana has\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_460\">                             407</span>\n\n\n\n\ndeposed that in Chickkaballapur town there is Mushin Shah\n\nDarga. A.13 had arranged some programs in that Darga and\n\nhe delivered speeches;    A.7, A.8, A.12 and A.13 used to\n\nattend the said programs along with others. In fact on this\n\naspect P.W.22 is not cross-examined at all.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_696\">370.        The evidences and proceedings referred supra\n\nclearly demonstrates that A.7 was a member of the\n\norganization.   He was regularly visiting Hyderabad, Nuzvid\n\nand Bangalore in connection with Ashram activities. He was\n\na Zonal Commander for Ashram activities and his elder\n\nbrother was the Commander-in-Chief. A.7 having worked in\n\nthe Indian Air Force, for 33 years has involved himself in\n\nseveral bomb blast cases and is already convicted for\n\ncriminal conspiracy to Islamize India.        A.6 is the elder\n\nbrother and A.8 and A.9 are the younger brothers of A.7. The\n\nevidence let in by the prosecution shows that A.7 was an\n\nactive participant in all the conspiracy meetings. He was\n\ndelivering the secret defence information to absconding A.1\n\nand his sons A.2 to A.5. Subsequent to the general meeting\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_461\">                               408</span>\n\n\n\n\non 29.10.1999, A.7 has attended several meetings at\n\nHyderabad, Bangalore, Bhatakurki and Nuzvid. However, in\n\nthe instant case, not only we have circumstantial evidence,\n\nbut also a direct evidence of P.W.54 as well as the\n\nconfessional statement of A.7.      A combined reading of all\n\nthese coupled with the answers given by A.7 at the time of\n\nexamination   under    <a href=\"/doc/767287/\" id=\"a_372\">Section      313</a>   of   Cr.P.C.,   amply\n\ndemonstrates that     the charges levelled against A.7 are\n\nproved beyond reasonable doubt.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_697\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_698\">371.        It is clear from the above that, the aim and\n\nobjective of Deendar Anjuman Ashram as per its founder late\n\nHazrath Siddiqe was to Islamise whole of the world including\n\nwhole India and therefore, the said statement proves beyond\n\nreasonable doubt that A.7 is a party to the conspiracy\n\nhatched for islamizing entire India. Therefore his contention\n\nthat he is falsely implicated in the case and he is very\n\ninnocent is patently false.   In fact, in the 313 statement\n\nwhen these incriminating materials were put to A.7, to give\n\nan opportunity to explain, but he has not offered any\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_462\">                              409</span>\n\n\n\n\nexplanation at all. Since, these incriminating materials are\n\nclearly established and based on the legal evidence, the trial\n\nCourt has recorded the finding that the Prosecution has\n\nproved the involvement of A.7 in the conspiracy leading to\n\nthe bomb blast and charges levelled against him to be true,\n\nthe same cannot be found fault with.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_699\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_700\">372.        A.8 is the brother of deceased A.6, A.7 and A.9.\n\nAll these four brothers were active members of Deendar\n\nAnjuman Organization. A.8 has a daughter. She was given\n\nin marriage to one Sheik Iqbal Ahmed, who was A.8 in\n\nHyderabad case in Crime No.35/2000.         A.8 was also an\n\naccused in that case and was convicted for the offence of\n\ncriminal conspiracy to Islamize the whole of India.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_701\">373.        P.W-54, Abdul Gaffoor has deposed before the\n\nCourt that A.6, A.7 and A.8 used to come to Deendar\n\nAnjuman Ashram from Nuzvid for Urs. A.8&#x27;s elder brother\n\nA.7 in his confession statement has categorically stated that\n\nhis grand father, father and his brothers are all followers of\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_463\">                              410</span>\n\n\n\n\nDeendar Anjuman Ashram located at Hyderabad and that in\n\nthe 3rd evening of January 1991, when he and his brothers\n\nwere   chitchatting   with   their   friends   regarding   Urs,\n\nMohinuddin came to A.6 and told him that Hazrath was\n\ncalling A.7 and A.8 from rear gate to his house.      A.7 also\n\nstates in the confessional statement that when he rang up\n\nA.8, he was told that they had done Sarria in Thirvur area,\n\nas such the patrolling was in full swing in Thirvur and that\n\nhe would call after three/four days. When A.7 went on leave\n\nin March, he came to know that Sarria was done by Tayyab\n\n(Hyderabad), Waheb (Vijayawada); the money was brought\n\nin a two-wheeler by Bank Staff; handkerchief was shown to\n\nthe party, doing Sarria from behind and a Tata Sumo was\n\nkept ready by driver Habib and A.8. After hitting the driver\n\nof two-wheeler, they snatched the suitcase from the clerk\n\nand went to Tata Sumo, which was standing about a\n\nkilometre near by. Tayyab got into Tata Sumo with the suit\n\ncase while the scooter went towards Vijayawada; the money\n\nwas kept in Hasan Mastry&#x27;s house. He also deposed that A.8\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_464\">                              411</span>\n\n\n\n\nwas present in the house of deceased A.6, where other co-\n\naccused persons were present. Later they all went to mango\n\ngrove situated near Nuzvid where a demonstration was held\n\nby A.9 with regard to preparation and blasting of bomb.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_702\">374.        In his Section 313 statement A.8 has pleaded\n\ncomplete ignorance about the alleged offence and claimed\n\nthat he served as conductor in APSRTC from 1975 till\n\n16.07.2000. He had been to Krishna Lanka police station to\n\nenquire why his name appeared in E Nadu Telugu News\n\nPaper, and police made him to wait in the police station. He\n\nhas been practicing as Unani and Ayurvedic medicine\n\nwithout a proper registration.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_703\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_704\">375.        A.9 is the younger brother of A.6 to A.8. A.7 in\n\nhis confession statement at Ex.P-245 has stated that A.9\n\nwas sent to Mardan, Pakistan to receive training in arms,\n\nammunitions and explosives and had been to various places\n\nin Karnataka along with other accused to survey suitable\n\nplaces for committing Sarria. They robbed a Gramin Bank\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_465\">                              412</span>\n\n\n\n\nemployee to mobilize the required fund. They also tried to\n\ncommit robbery at Sangli but not successful. A.9 had also\n\nattended secret conspiracy meetings in the flat of A.19. He\n\nvisited Bhatakurki and other places along with the elders of\n\nthe Deendar Anjuman Organisation,        took oath at A.6&#x27;s\n\nhand. A.9 demonstrated gelatin sticks, detonators and fuse\n\nwire with pistol and he burnt the IFT fuse wire at a Mango\n\ngrove near Nuzvid and assured A.11 that he would fix a\n\ntimer in the bomb. A.9 prepared a time bomb together with\n\none Maqbool and later it was planted in Machilipatnam. A.9\n\nwent to Huzurnagar and brought local made explosive\n\npowder and gave it to Anwar@ Hanif to keep in his house\n\nalong with one round of .303 which A.7 had purchased from\n\nDevanandam in 1998.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_705\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_706\">376.        A.9 also narrated details of mandir programme\n\ntaught by A.2; and brought time bombs. When A.9 took 1\n\nft. wire and burnt it, A.11 was watching time in his wrist\n\nwatch and calculated that it took 12 seconds to burn that\n\nwire and within that time one could walk 60 ft. to 80 ft. away\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_466\">                                413</span>\n\n\n\n\nafter lighting it. Then A.9 assured all the members present\n\nthere that he would use the timer in preparing bombs and\n\nbrought the timer together with one Maqbool and showed to\n\nall the members including A.10 and A.11. Thus it is clear\n\nthat A.9 beng trained in preparation of bombs at Mardan in\n\nPakistan, attended several conspiracy meetings both have\n\nprepared time bombs together with Maqbool and Bajeed,\n\nplanted and exploded the same in Churches and Temples.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_707\">377.        A.11 on the date of his arrest was aged about 53\n\nyears and worked as Administrative Assistant in BEML,\n\nBangalore, having his own residential house at Vijayanagara,\n\nBangalore City.    He was residing along with his wife and\n\nchildren.       In his statement under Section 313, he has\n\nunequivocally admitted that twice he had visited Deendhar\n\nAshram situated at Asif Nagar, Hyderabad, like his any other\n\nvisits to other Shrines like Ajameer, etc., along with family\n\nmembers.        He knows about the principle of universal\n\nbrotherhood       propogated     by   Deendhar     Anjuman\n\nOrganization;    during Sarvadharma Sammelana, Deendhar\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_467\">                                  414</span>\n\n\n\n\nAnjuman preachers gave lecture on these principles, i.e.,\n\nexistence of only one God. So he has been worshipping that\n\none God by thousands of names and following that\n\nprocedure.    He has gone through so many religious books\n\nmentioned in that Ashram and developed love and affection\n\nfor all persons. Therefore it is not in dispute that he is the\n\nmember of Deendhar Anjuman Organization and he has\n\nvisited the Deendhar Anjuman Ashram situated at Asifnagar,\n\nHyderabad.      This accused is convicted for the offence of\n\nconspiracy by the Hyderabad Court and he was arrayed as\n\nAccused No.45 in that case.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_708\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_709\">378.          P.W-5-Mohd. Zia Ur Rehman was working in\n\nEducation Department as First Division Assistant.             He\n\nretired in the year 1990. He is the father of A.15, who died\n\nin a bomb blast at Magadi Road. He admitted that in 1990\n\nafter   his   retirement,   he   attended   Urs   conducted   at\n\nHyderabad Deendhar Anjuman Ashram. Again in 1992 he\n\nattended the said Urs at Hyderabad. During that period he\n\nrequested A.1 to visit his house in Bangalore. Accordingly in\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_468\">                             415</span>\n\n\n\n\n1992 A.1 came to Bangalaore and he had gone to railway\n\nstation and received him.   He took A.1 to his house; A.1\n\nstayed there for a one day and one night. At that timeA.11\n\nto A.13 visited A.1 along with their family members.\n\nDeendhar Anjuman Organization has published certain\n\nbooks and he has read some four or five books, so published\n\nby the organization.    There is a library in the Ashram.\n\nDuring 1994, when he had gone to Urs at Hyderabad, A.1 to\n\nA.13 also had come there.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_710\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_711\">379.        P.W-5 has also given particulars of persons who\n\nwere the presidents of the organization from time to time;\n\nthat they too requested permission to carry on the activities\n\nin Bangalore. They advised them to have a programme by\n\nname Sari Darshe Quran and asked him to be the secretary.\n\nAccordingly on first Sunday of every month they started\n\nprogramme in his house. A.11 and A.13 were attending the\n\nsaid programme along with their family members. A.10 also\n\nhad come to his house on some occasions. In 1999 when he\n\nand A.12 went to Urs at Hyderabad, they put forth a\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_469\">                              416</span>\n\n\n\n\nproposal to perform his son&#x27;s marriage in the presence of\n\nA.1, for which A.1 suggested that his son could be married to\n\nTajdar Begaum, the daughter of A.12. A.10 was a friend of\n\nP.W.5&#x27;s son and used to go to their house to meet P.W.5&#x27;s\n\nson initially on a scooter and later in a blue-black van.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_712\">380.        In the cross examination of P.W.5 it is elicited\n\nthat after the bomb blast, the Urs at Hyderabad was\n\nstopped. In April 2002, the Central Government banned the\n\norganization and therefore Urs is not conducted. A.11 and\n\nall persons who attended Darshe Quran in his house\n\ncondemned the bomb blast;       A.10 used to attend the said\n\nprogramme alone and leave the place immediately after\n\nDarshe Quran was over.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_713\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_714\">381.        P.W-6 Haseeb Ahamad is the father-in-law of\n\nA.14. He gave his daughter Harpathunnisa in marriage to\n\nA.14, who was residing at Hyderabad. P.W.6 deposed that\n\nhis son-in-law A.14 died in a bomb blast near Minerva Mills.\n\nHe also had gone to Urs at Hyderabad in 1998 and 1999.\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_470\">                              417</span>\n\n\n\n\nThe marriage of his daughter with A.14 took place in a mass\n\nmarriage     at   Deendhar   Anjuman   Ashram,    Hyderabad.\n\nA.1 was present at that time and blessed the couple. He did\n\nnot verify A.14&#x27;s past and other particulars.    The house of\n\nP.W-5 is situated in J.C. Nagar and therefore he and his\n\nfamily members used to go to P.W.5&#x27;s house to attend the\n\nfunctions.    A.10, A.11 and A.12 used to come with their\n\nfamily members to the house of P.W-5.         They were also\n\ncoming to Urs at Hyderabad. After the marriage, his son-in-\n\nlaw had come to his house thrice along with his daughter.\n\nThe day prior to the bomb blast, A.14 had come to his house\n\nwith his daughter. His daughter and his wife went to attend\n\na marriage of their relative at J.C. Nagar.     He did not go\n\nbecause he was not well. His son-in-law-A.14 also did not go\n\nto the marriage. Though his son-in-law took the address of\n\nthe place of marriage, he did not go there, but went\n\nsomewhere else. At mid-night, the police informed them that\n\nhis son-in-law died in an accident.    He did not go to the\n\nhospital as he was not well.       His wife went to Victoria\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_471\">                                  418</span>\n\n\n\n\nHospital. There she came to know that A.14 died in a bomb\n\nblast.   Two    months   after    the   incident,   his   daughter\n\ncommitted suicide because of her husband&#x27;s involvement in\n\nbomb blast. This witness has also deposed in the Hyderabad\n\ncase.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_715\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_716\">382.           P.W-64 - M.B. The\n\ncontents of the floppys were not shown to him. Whether they\n\nwere blank or not was also not known to him. His signature\n\nwas taken on visiting card and pamphlets. Therefore, the calls made by\n\nA.11 to various co-accused persons so many times shows\n\nthe meeting of minds of the co-accused persons. A.11 has\n\nnot explained anything about these telephone calls made to\n\nco-accused persons during his examination under Section\n\n313 Cr.P.C.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_726\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_727\">389.          Further P.W-57 has produced the call details in\n\nbetween the mobile phone No.9844006108 belonging to P.W-\n\n13 Mohammed Kasim Shet who is the younger brother of\n\nA.11 and Syed Siddique Hussain Mulla having mobile\n\nNo.9844115070 as per       Ex.P-122 and Ex.P-123.    A.11 on\n\n01.08.2000 led P.W-64 and his staff and panchas to a\n\nmango grove purchased by deceased A.6. Mulla.\n\nA.10, A.13 and the sons of A.1 to discuss about spreading of\n\nIslam throughout India. Ex.P.254 is drawn in this connection at Mango\n\ngrove. A.7 in his confessional statement at Ex.P-245\n\nhas extensively referred to the role of A.11 in the conspiracy.\n\nAt para.21, he has referred to the presence of A.11 in the 3rd\n\nday of Urs in September 1999 at 7.40 hours at the premises\n\nof Deendar Anjuman Ashram, Asif Nagar, Hyderabad. At para.28 he has referred to the presence of\n\nA.11 in their meeting at Nuzvid in the last week of April\n\n2000, in the Mango Garden. The garden watchman was sent\n\nfor tea and then gelatin sticks, detonators and fuse wire with\n\npistol was shown to them.     There when IFT fuse wire was\n\nburnt by A.9, A.11 was watching his wrist watch plus\n\nrunning. Around 60 to 80 feet fast ward was done and it\n\ntook about 9 to 12 seconds for burning the IFT fuse wire.\n\nAfter the demonstration, they proposed that if there was a\n\ntimer, then it would be better. Then, they all came back\n\nhome, discussed about Farooq program, which was brought\n\nfrom A.1. in March 2000.     A.7 and A.11 went to internet\n\nshop and checked for the mail in their inbox and sent one\n\nmail to A.1 about the health of A.6&#x27;s son and waited for the\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_482\">                             429</span>\n\n\n\n\nreply. At para.30 of the confessional statement he has\n\nstated that A.6 and A.7 proposed to go to Bangalore and\n\nBhatakurki and accordingly both of them started for\n\nBangalore by private bus in the last or second week of May,\n\n2000. They went to A.11&#x27;s house directly, where they showed\n\nthe papers of A.18. A.7 had one old telephone directory of\n\nIOC with some E-mail addresses also.          The telephone\n\nnumbers and A.18&#x27;s message were scanned and floppies\n\nwere made. Thereafter, they went to nearby internet shop\n\ntrying to send e-mail on J.H.M.@USA.net, but due to some\n\ntechnical fault, they could not do it. As it was around 20.30\n\nhours, they came back to A.11&#x27;s house, had dinner and left\n\nfor Bhatkurki by Hubli fast passenger. On 22nd May, 2000 at\n\nabout 22.00 hours, on their way to Hyderabad, they rang up\n\nA.10 and A.11, requesting them to come and meet them\n\nimmediately. A.10 and A.11 came the next day and met in\n\nAnwar@Hanif&#x27;s house and were told that if anybody does\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_483\">                                 430</span>\n\n\n\n\nSarria, that amount should be completely handed-over to\n\nA.6 after deducting their expenditure, if required and that all\n\nhave to work collectively without any misunderstanding.\n\nLater A.11 spoke to A.6 for half an hour and others were also\n\npresent in that meeting. On 21st June, 2000, A.7 along with\n\nother accused was coming to Bangalore; A.18 rang up A.10\n\nand A.11 and told them that A.2 was coming and that they\n\nshould be at A.10&#x27;s house. All of them met at A.10&#x27;s house.\n\nA.18 brought a chart of duties to be carried out by each\n\nindividual, which had few columns of training the persons.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_736\">395.        A.12 was also called as Bade Mulla.            His\n\nyounger brother P.W-7 Ahmed Hussain Mulla was called as\n\nChote Mulla. P.W-10-Syed Mohammad Hussain is the son of\n\nA.12.   He has deposed that A.12 is his father and P.W.9 is\n\nhis mother. He admits that prior to the year 2000,\n\nthere was a computer in their house. It was not working\n\nproperly and the police have seized it.   He was treated as\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_486\">                             433</span>\n\n\n\n\nhostile witness and cross examined by the Public Prosecutor.\n\nFrom the aforesaid evidence it is clear that he admits that\n\nhis parents were visiting Deendhar Ashram at Hyderabad.\n\nThere was a computer in his house and it was seized by the\n\npolice.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_742\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_743\">399.          P.W-6 has deposed that house of P.W-5 is at\n\nJ.C. Nagar.     He and his family members were attending\n\nprogramme in his house.     A.10, A.11 and A.12 were also\n\nattending the programme in the house of P.W-5 with their\n\nfamily members. P.W-21-Srinivas is aged about 41 years and a\n\ncobbler by profession.    He has deposed that he knows\n\nMohisin Sab and his son Siddique-A.15 in this case. A.15\n\nhad a STD shop in church road.     He used to spend some\n\ntime in the said shop. He knows A.15 for the last 15 years\n\nand he was A.15&#x27;s friend. The house of A.15 was in front of\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_487\">                               434</span>\n\n\n\n\nhis house.   Every Sunday in the morning, afternoon and\n\nevening, Molwi Sab and other friends used to come there.\n\nAbout 15 to 20 persons were coming for the programme.\n\nWhen enquired, he was informed him that they were all\n\ncoming for the programme and A.15 invited him also for the\n\nprogramme.    He came to know that A.15 died in a bomb\n\nblast. When the police showed him the photo album in the\n\npolice station as per Ex.P-28, he identified three persons i.e.,\n\nA.11, A.12 and A.13.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_746\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_747\">401.         On 15.07.2000, P.W-64 went to the house of\n\nA.12 at No.49, 5th cross, Gundappa Street, Rehmathnagar,\n\nR.T. Nagar, 1st main road, Bangalore. In the presence of\n\npanchas, he searched his house and found M.O-13 to M.O-\n\n29 and it was seized under mahazar Ex.P-80 and Ex.P-247.\n\nOn 28.07.2000 A.12 led the Investigating Officer, his staff\n\nand panchas, namely Venkatesh and Shankar Rao, to the\n\nhouse, situated at R.T. Nagar, Gundappa Street, 1st Main, 5th\n\nCorss, Bangalore. A.12 produced 16 incriminating articles\n\nbefore the panchas and drew the mahazar Ex.P-249. Again,\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_488\">                               435</span>\n\n\n\n\non 29.07.2000, A.12 led P.W-64 - Investigating Officer, his\n\nstaff   and   panchas   P.W-51-G.    Vishwanath   and        N.\n\nVenkatesh, to his work place situated in BEML factory,\n\nBangalore.     P.W-51 has deposed that A.12 was a lathe\n\nmachine operator. P.W-22 is one L. Lakshminarayana.           He is the\n\nresident of Chikkaballapur, Kolar District.         He is a Timber\n\nmerchant and has identified A.13. The role of A.12 in the\n\nconspiracy and acts in pursuance of the same are clearly set\n\nout in the confessional statement of A.7, which is marked as\n\nEx.P-245.   At para.27 of the confessional statement, it is\n\nstated that in the third week of April, A.6 and A.7 came to\n\nhouse of A.11, where they made a new floppy of unit location\n\nfrom outside. A.13 was aged about 55 years on the date of the\n\nincident.    He is the owner of readymade garment shop at\n\nChikkaballapur and was residing there along with his wife\n\nand children. A.19, resident of Deendar Anjuman Ashram,\n\nAsifnagar, Hyderabad is the son-in-law of this A.13. In the\n\nsecond week of April, 2000, A.6 proposed to go to Bangalore\n\nto meet P.W.7.      A.7 and A.6 started in the evening to\n\nChikkaballapur     where   A.13    resided.   They    reached\n\nChickkaballapur next evening.       A.6 did not know P.W.7.\n\nA.13 rang up to P.W.7 and told that A.6 was bringing a\n\nmessage from from A.1 and that A.13 would try to come by\n\nearly hours i.e., 6.00 a.m. A.13 also rang up to A.11 and told\n\nthat A.6     was coming over there.   Next day, they reached\n\nP.W.7&#x27;s house. After reaching Nuzvid, A.13 was asked to go\n\nto Nuzvid.    After two or three days, A.13 came to Nuzvid.\n\nThen A.6 asked him about procuring of revolvers. A.13 told\n\nthat he had to go to Calcutta to search for someone who can\n\nget revolvers. A.13 was paid Rs.3,000/- and was told that if\n\nrevolvers were available, then he had to give a call and that\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_492\">                              439</span>\n\n\n\n\nthe money would be sent. After three days, A.13 gave a call\n\nand told that he could not trace the person, but his family\n\nrequired Rs.1,000/- urgently and requested for TMO. After\n\ntwo or three days, A.6 sent the TMO to A.13&#x27;s family.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_753\">405.        As per the directions of P.W-64, Inspector R.N.\n\nNataraj went to the house of A.13, along with A.13, situated\n\nat Chikkaballapur and seized incriminating articles M.O.30\n\nto M.O.37. A copy of the seizure mahazar is marked as Ex.P-\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_754\">81.    The Prosecution examined the panch P.W-50 namely\n\nD.N. Munikrishnappa.      He is aged about 50 years and\n\nagriculturist by profession. He is the resident of Dinnahalli,\n\nChikkaballapur   Taluk.     On     23.07.2000,   he   came   to\n\nChikkaballapur for his work.        At about 4.00 p.m. he\n\naccompanied the COD Police Inspector, Nataraj and his staff\n\nto the house of A.13. The\n\nwarning letters, book containing the literature of Deendar\n\nAnjuman Ashram, copy of the order passed by District\n\nMagistrate, Dharwad against late Hazarath Siddique, shows\n\nthat A.13 is also an active member of Deendar Anjuman\n\nAshram, Hyderabad.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_756\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_757\">406.        P.W-5 in his evidence has deposed that A13 was\n\nattending the meeting in his house at Bangalore. In\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_494\">                              441</span>\n\n\n\n\nfront of that house, a Mosque is situated. On the same day A.13 led\n\nP.W64 and his staff to room Nos.2 and 3 of Ashoka Lodge\n\nsituated at Hyderabd and showed those rooms as the places\n\nwhere deceased A.6 held conspiracy meetings with others\n\nunder his leadership.   The manager of the lodge identified\n\nA.11 and A.13. Therefore, one more mahazar was drawn as\n\nper Ex.P-258. Therefore he identified A.13 before the Court.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_758\">407.          In the confession statement of A.7 as per\n\nEx.P.245, there is a reference to the role of A.13 in the\n\nconspiracy.    A.7 has stated that on the third day of Urs,\n\naround 7.30 hours, he and his elder brother reached\n\nDeendar Anjuman Ashram, Asif Nagar.         They found that\n\nalready some members were sitting there.      By 7.40 hours\n\neverybody came inside the room.      A.1 was sitting on the\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_495\">                             442</span>\n\n\n\n\ncarpet and by his side A.3 was standing; the other members\n\nincluding A.13 were sitting. In that meeting A.1 told them\n\nthat A.6 would be incharge for all operations and he would\n\ngive instructions regarding the work to be done. Even\n\nmessages if any would be conveyed through him. That after\n\nRamzan festival, A.13 came to Nuzvid to know about the\n\nprogram.    From there, they went to Vijayawada.       A.7&#x27;s\n\nbrother and A.13 told that first priority is money. For that\n\nthey had to plan Sarria.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_759\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_760\">408.        From the above, it is clear that A.13 was a\n\nmember of the Deendar Anjuman Ashram.            He was in\n\npossession of the literature and publications of the said\n\nAshram.    A.19 his son-in-law was residing in the Deendar\n\nAnjuman Ashram, Asif Nagar, Hyderabad. M.Os. 30 to 37\n\nwere seized from his trunk and almirahs.          A.13 was\n\nattending the meetings of the Ashram at Hyderabad,\n\nBangalore and Bhatakurki.    He was also attending Urs at\n\nHyderabad as found in the confession statement of A.7. He\n\nwas also actively involved in planning Sarria,    procuring\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_496\">                               443</span>\n\n\n\n\nrevolvers.    For that purpose, he even went to Calcutta.\n\nTherefore, the complicity of A.13 in commission of the\n\naforesaid offence is clearly proved.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_761\">\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_762\">409.          P.W-31-Raheel   Ahamed,   son   of   P.W-6   has\n\ndeposed that his sister Harpathunissa, wife of deceased A.14\n\ndied about 6-7 years back and A.14 died in Bangalore in\n\nbomb explosion. P.W.31&#x27;s mother P.W-32 and wife of P.W-6\n\nhas spoken about the marriage of her daughter with A.14 in\n\nthe Deendar Anjuman Ashram, Hyderabad that her son-in-\n\nlaw A.14 died in explosion which took place in the car.\n\nP.W.19, P.W.21, also have identified A.14 in the photo\n\nalbum.       P.W.3 - Mukunda in Ex.P-5 photo album had\n\nidentified A.14. In Ex.P-7 Nanjundaiah identified A.14 in the\n\npresence of P.W-4 as per panchanam Ex.P-9.\n\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"CDiscource\" id=\"p_763\">410.          P.W-54 has deposed that in November 1999,\n\nA.25 sent a person by name Hussain on motor cycle and told\n\nthat it should be given to another person who would come\n\nasking for the vehicle. Four days later A.14 came and took\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_497\">                              444</span>\n\n\n\n\nthe motor cycle.
